% Keywords below:

\begin{abstract}
Euclidean Condensate Theory (ECT) proposes that Lorentzian spacetime,
quantum mechanics, and gravitational dynamics emerge from spontaneous
$O(4)\to O(3)$ symmetry breaking of a scalar condensate field~$\Phi$
on a four-dimensional Euclidean manifold.

\paragraph{Foundational architecture.}
ECT is not formulated by postulating the observed macroscopic world
directly. Instead, it starts from a more primitive Euclidean
$\Phi$-medium and formalises a minimal set of structural assumptions
about that medium. These assumptions are organised into two layers.
\textbf{P1--P3} define the microscopic level: a four-dimensional
Euclidean arena, an $O(4)$-invariant local action, and a minimal
real-scalar microdynamics.
\textbf{P4} specifies that the physically realised vacuum belongs to
an ordered branch with nonzero gradient structure, thereby allowing the
spontaneous breaking $O(4)\to O(3)$ without introducing explicit
anisotropy into the bare micro-action.
From this ordered branch there emerge two effective developments:
the \emph{geometric branch} (macroscopic causal and gravitational
physics) and the \emph{coherent-topological branch} (the pre-quantum
sector). The smooth coherent topological sector is not introduced as an
independent fundamental postulate; rather, its winding structure is a
derived consequence of the existence of a single-valued collective phase
field in the ordered broken phase. Likewise, the physical viability of
the macroscopic branch is not imposed as a separate axiom, but analysed
through the broken-phase kinetic structure, signature classification,
and the Lorentzian viability window developed later in Part~I.

\paragraph{ECT basics: what is established.}
The foundational sections establish:
Lorentzian signature for $\alpha>\beta$ with effective speed
$c_*=\sqrt{\beta/(\alpha-\beta)}$~(Level~A in broken-phase EFT);
the broken-phase excitation spectrum (radial, orientation, phase modes);
the parameter hierarchy across four levels (bare, vacuum, EFT, coherent);
the gravitational stiffness scale $M_G\equiv\bar M_{\rm Pl}$
matched to Newton's constant via $G_N=c_*^4/(8\pi M_G^2)$~(Level~B: EFT matching);
the pre-quantum action scale $S_0$ from the winding condition
$S_{\rm loop,min}=2\pi S_0$~(elementary-loop action scale within the smooth coherent branch BR1; matched to $\hbar$ at Level~B);
and the retarded causal kernel $G_{\rm ret}(t,\mathbf x)=0$ for $t<0$
strictly proved in the quadratic sector~(Level~A).

\paragraph{Subsequent developments.}
The quantum and gravitational branches are developed in later sections.
The Quantum Sector derives canonical commutation relations from condensate
phase single-valuedness, the Klein--Gordon and Schrödinger equations via
analytic continuation, and the identification $S_0=\hbar$ from loop
quantisation~(Level~B; self-consistency condition, not zero-parameter prediction).
An internal consistency relation linking $G_N$ and $\hbar$ follows at Level~B.
The arrow of time is supported by a Level~A directional backbone
(retarded propagation, dissipative orientation, directed decoherence).
The thermodynamic second law arises as a Level~B result within the
effective ohmic/Markov closure, with a structurally motivated
low-entropy initial condition from P4+BR1.
The Born measure is established under structural assumptions~C1--C3
(Level~B; unique outcome selection remains open).
Linearised and generalised Einstein equations are established in the
Macroscopic Physics section via the $\phi$-first scalar-tensor closure~(Level~B; homogeneous pure-metric limit uniquely Einstein--Hilbert by
Lovelock uniqueness under A1--A5).

\paragraph{Gauge and matter sectors.}
Structural embeddings of $U(1)$, $SU(2)$, $SU(3)$ gauge fields emerge
via condensate phase symmetries (partial; full SM derivation open).
Fermionic structure is reconstructed from spinorial representations
of the ordered $O(3)$ sector~(Level~B; structural compatibility,
not a microscopic derivation).
A candidate fifth interaction appears at Planck-suppressed strength.

\paragraph{Macroscopic applications.}
Flat galactic rotation curves arise via the macroscopic amplitude
variable of the ordered branch,
$\phi=\frac{1}{\beta}\ln(u/u_\infty)$, whose critical branch
($\mathcal{F}\propto Y_\phi^{3/2}$) yields MOND-like dynamics and the
baryonic Tully--Fisher relation as exact algebraic consequences
(exploratory SPARC fits: $\chi^2/N\sim 1.8$--$11.5$);
a quasi-universal acceleration scale $g_\dagger\propto cH_0$
(new testable prediction);
pre-Lorentzian branch-selection as the structural ECT mechanism
for horizon and flatness problems; an ECT-native computation of
$n_s$ and $r$ is in progress~(Open, OP3);
dark energy as residual condensate energy (equation-of-state
compatible with a $w_0>-1$ value; numerical closure depends on
unconstrained condensate parameters);
and a soft orientation sector whose dark-matter role remains open
(no particle dark matter is required by the present formulation).
Black-hole implications of the ordered condensate
branch are exploratory~(Level~B).

\paragraph{Epistemic status.}
All results are classified by level of rigour: Level~A (strict EFT or
quadratic-sector derivations), Level~B (matching, effective, or structural
arguments), Level~C (speculative).
Open problems (three fermion generations, $SU(3)$ origin, fine-structure
constant, gradient condensate derivation from P3) are stated precisely.
ECT is not presented as a complete theory of everything, but as a coherent
and testable framework in which relativistic spacetime, quantum mechanics,
and gravity emerge from a single scalar condensate.
\end{abstract}

cosmology;
modified gravity;
SPARC;
Casimir effect;
Unruh effect;
black-hole thermodynamics
