  (any $J(\omega)\geq 0$, no ohmic/Markov)
  & A & Bochner; quadratic sector;
    with the causal-kernel identification
    discussed below; App.~\ref{app:directed_decoherence} \\
A6: Single ordered branch realised
  & A & P4: branch selection \\
Low-entropy interpretation of initial branch
  & B & Depends on coarse graining \\
Retarded structure of ordered $\Phi$-branch
  & A & App.~\ref{app:causal_hyperbolic} \\
First-principles derivation of causal FV
  environment kernel
  & C (open) & OP1, OP5 \\
Strict monotonicity $dS/dw\geq 0$ at all times
  & B & Requires Markov;
    \S\ref{sec:second_law_basics} \\
Full microscopic H-theorem
  & C (open) & OP1, OP5, OP24 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{center}


\subsection{The second law of thermodynamics in ECT}
\label{sec:second_law_basics}

\textit{Status: layered.
The directional architecture of the arrow of time is
Level~A~(Section~\ref{sec:arrow_of_time}).
The thermodynamic second law $dS_{\rm ent}/dw\geq 0$ for
macroscopic systems is Level~B: it requires the effective
ohmic and Markovian closure in addition to the Level~A backbone.
The full microscopic H-theorem from bare postulates alone remains
open~(OP1, OP5, OP24).}

The thermodynamic second law in ECT is not postulated.
It is built on the Level~A directional architecture of
Section~\ref{sec:arrow_of_time} together with two additional
effective-closure assumptions.
The present section develops the Level~B part of the story.

\paragraph{The second law as a Level~B extension of the arrow of time.}
Section~\ref{sec:arrow_of_time} established that in the quadratic
open-system reduction the irreversible kernel is positive semidefinite
($\Gamma_{\rm irr}\geq 0$; A5), so off-diagonal histories are
suppressed rather than amplified.
This gives the directionality of decoherence.
What it does not yet give is \emph{strict monotonicity} of a
coarse-grained entropy at every instant.
For a generic super-ohmic bath ($J(\omega)\propto\omega^4$, the
natural ECT scaling; Appendix~\ref{app:ohmic_markov}),
temporary partial recoherences are structurally possible.
The step from directionality to monotone entropy growth
requires two additional closure assumptions.

\paragraph{The Boltzmann analogy.}
The logical structure is directly parallel to Boltzmann's kinetic
theory: variational microdynamics of the condensate plus a single realised ordered
branch~(A6), interpreted as statistically exceptional once S6 and
coarse graining are taken into account, yields irreversible macroscopic
behaviour.
The Loschmidt tension is addressed at the same structural level as in
Boltzmann-type reasoning:
the microscopic dynamics remains reversible, but the realised initial
sector is statistically exceptional, so time-reversed macroscopic
histories are not typical.
What ECT adds beyond classical statistical mechanics is a structural
explanation of why the initial condition is special: it is tied to
ordered-branch selection, not imposed ad hoc.

\paragraph{Statistical speciality of the ordered branch (Level~B).}
The structural property~S6 states that no configuration of the
$\Phi$-medium is privileged \emph{a priori}.
Against that background, the coherent ordered branch selected by P4 is
statistically special: it is a highly organised and constrained subset
of the full configuration space.
Disordered states are expected to dominate the admissible state count,
while the realised ordered branch corresponds to a highly constrained
subset, naturally interpretable as a low-entropy sector at the
coarse-grained level.
This argument is Level~B because it invokes state-counting without a
rigorous measure on configuration space.
It strengthens the structural motivation of~A6.

\paragraph{Entropy growth from inhomogeneous ordering (Level~B).}
The structural property~S8 states that ordering need not be globally
uniform: ordered domains, transition regions, local misalignments, and
slowly varying branch backgrounds are all admissible.
As the branch evolves, coarse-grained descriptions generically explore
a larger class of such partially ordered configurations than the
initially coherent state.
The number of coarse-grained states accessible through
domain-boundary structure is expected to increase as domains expand
and interact, supporting $dS_{\rm ent}/dw\geq 0$ at the
domain-structure level.
This is a structural Level~B heuristic, not a first-principles
derivation.

\paragraph{Topological defects and domain production (Level~B).}
The topological completeness property~S9 further strengthens this
picture.
The ordered branch may contain or generate domain walls, textures,
defect cores, or other transition structures.
Such objects are not external additions to the theory but admissible
configurations of the $\Phi$-medium
itself~(Section~\ref{sec:defect_sectors}).
Their formation, interaction, and coarse-grained proliferation provide
natural channels through which an initially coherent ordered state can
lose ideal uniformity and populate a broader macroscopic ensemble,
thereby supporting the thermodynamic arrow~(Level~B from S8+S9).

\paragraph{Two Level~B closure assumptions.}
To proceed quantitatively from the Level~A directional
results to strict entropy monotonicity, two effective working
assumptions are needed.
The scale analysis of Appendix~\ref{app:ohmic_markov} shows that
the standard 3D Goldstone-like environment gives a super-ohmic spectral
density $J(\omega)\propto\omega^4$.
A strict monotonicity statement requires an additional step:
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)]
  \item \emph{Effective ohmic regime:}
    \begin{equation}
      J(\omega) \approx N_{\rm eff}\,\gamma\,\omega,
      \label{eq:ohmic_3p9}
    \end{equation}
    adopted as a phenomenological reduction~(Level~B).
    This may arise from non-standard coupling form, reduced effective
    bath dimensionality, or coarse-graining.
  \item \emph{Markovian regime:}
    \begin{equation}
      \tau_{\rm env} \ll \tau_{\rm dec},
      \label{eq:markov_3p9}
    \end{equation}
    where $\tau_{\rm env}\sim m_\sigma^{-1}$ is the bath memory time
    and $\tau_{\rm dec} = 1/(N_{\rm eff}\gamma)$ is the decoherence
    time~(Level~B).
\end{enumerate}

\paragraph{Working entropy-growth law.}
Under the ohmic and Markovian
assumptions~\eqref{eq:ohmic_3p9}--\eqref{eq:markov_3p9}:
\begin{equation}
  \frac{dS_{\rm ent}}{dw}
  = N_{\rm eff}\,\gamma\!\left(\frac{dq}{dw}\right)^{\!2}
  \geq 0.
  \label{eq:dSdw_second_law}
\end{equation}
The sign is anchored in the Level~A retarded structure;
the explicit form requires the Level~B closure~(see
Section~\ref{sec:influence_functional} for the full derivation).

\paragraph{Statistical suppression of time-reversed trajectories.}
\begin{equation}
  \frac{P_{\rm back}}{P_{\rm fwd}}
  = \exp\!\left(-2N_{\rm eff}\gamma\,\Delta w
    \cdot\frac{(\Delta q)^2}{\ell^2}\right).
  \label{eq:PbackPfwd}
\end{equation}
For a macroscopic system ($1\,{\rm mol}$, $T=300\,{\rm K}$,
$\Delta w = c\cdot 1\,{\rm ns}$): exponent
$\approx 3.6\times10^{33}$, so
$P_{\rm back}/P_{\rm fwd}\approx 0$.
For $N_{\rm eff}\sim 1$: exponent $\sim 10^{-15}$, quantum
reversibility recovered.
The second law is statistical rather than absolute: it holds
overwhelmingly for macroscopic systems and naturally weakens for
quantum-scale ones.

\paragraph{Three regimes.}
\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{Cosmological:} ordered branch defines cosmic arrow;
    thermodynamic arrow is effectively exact.
  \item \textbf{Macroscopic~($N_{\rm eff}\gg 1$):} reverse trajectories
    exponentially suppressed; second law holds to overwhelming accuracy.
  \item \textbf{Quantum~($N_{\rm eff}\sim 1$):} fine-grained phases not
    washed out; effective reversibility recovered.
\end{itemize}
This three-regime picture is consistent with the $S_0$-controlled
divide~(Section~\ref{sec:hbar_status}): when action differences between
competing histories satisfy $|\Delta S|\sim S_0$, relative phases
cannot be suppressed and the classical irreversible description breaks
down.

\paragraph{What remains open.}
\begin{itemize}
  \item A full microscopic H-theorem from bare postulates
    alone~(OP1, OP5, OP24).
  \item A first-principles derivation of the ohmic and Markovian closure
    from the condensate microstructure~(OP-Q15).
  \item A first-principles derivation of the causal environment
    kernel $K(\tau<0)=0$ from ECT microdynamics~(OP1, OP5).
  \item Entropy production in strongly nonlinear branch dynamics.
  \item A rigorous measure on the configuration space (which would
    promote the state-counting argument from Level~B to Level~A).
\end{itemize}

\paragraph{Status summary.}

(from Section~\ref{sec:arrow_of_time}).}

\subsection{Comparison with other theories}
\label{sec:comparison_theories}

\paragraph{Einstein--aether theory~\cite{Jacobson2004}.}
In Einstein--aether theory the anisotropy is introduced by a postulated
aether vector field $u^A$; it does not arise spontaneously.
In ECT the orientation variable $n_A$ is a collective variable of the
broken phase---it enters the kinetic EFT tensor, not the bare action P3.
The anisotropy is entirely spontaneous.
A systematic comparison of emergent-gravity approaches is given in
Section~\ref{sec:graviton}.

\paragraph{Ho\v{r}ava--Lifshitz gravity.}
This theory introduces explicit anisotropy between space and time
by construction, breaking Lorentz invariance explicitly at the
fundamental level. In ECT Lorentz invariance of the fundamental
action is preserved (P2) and broken only spontaneously.

\paragraph{CDT (Causal Dynamical Triangulations)~\cite{Ambjorn2005}.}
In CDT the Lorentzian structure is imposed as a constraint on
the lattice geometry. In ECT geometry is secondary to the field~$\Phi$
and the metric is emergent.

\paragraph{Hartle--Hawking no-boundary proposal~\cite{HartleHawking1983}.}
Both approaches use a Euclidean arena, but for Hartle--Hawking it is a
computational tool for defining path integrals. In ECT the Euclidean
manifold is the fundamental arena, not a mathematical device.

\noindent\textbf{Summary.}
In ECT the Lorentzian signature and physical time arise from the
$O(4)\to O(3)$ ordering of a single scalar vacuum sector; the arrow
of time is strongly motivated by the same structure and becomes
quantitative in the decoherence analysis~(Section~\ref{sec:influence_functional}).
Unlike Einstein--aether~\cite{Jacobson2004} and Horava--Lifshitz~\cite{Horava2009}, the anisotropy is
entirely spontaneous. Unlike CDT, geometry is secondary to field~$\Phi$.
Unlike Hartle--Hawking, the Euclidean arena is fundamental.

An additional ECT-specific feature distinguishes it from generic
internal-symmetry-breaking theories: because the ordered quantity
$Q_A=\partial_A\Phi$ is an exact one-form rather than an independent
multiplet, the orientation sector is subject to an integrability
constraint that reduces the naive $O(4)/O(3)$ Goldstone content
(Appendix~\ref{app:goldstone_integrability}).  This has no analogue
in standard internal SSB frameworks.


% ============================================================
% SECTION 4: SPECTRUM AND EFFECTIVE DYNAMICS OF THE BROKEN PHASE
% ============================================================
\section{Spectrum and Effective Dynamics of the Broken Phase}
\label{sec:broken_spectrum}

After postulate~P4 has selected the gradient-ordered vacuum sector
and the derived coherent-sector rule~BR1 has identified the smooth
coherent topological sector, the next step is to determine which
excitations exist above this vacuum and what their effective dynamics is.

\subsection{Polar decomposition and collective variables}
\label{sec:polar_decomp}

The background field is given by postulate~P4:
\begin{equation}
  \Phi_{\rm bg}(X) = u_0\,w + \Phi_*,\qquad \partial_A\Phi_{\rm bg} = u_0\,\delta_{Aw}.
\end{equation}
In the broken phase the polar decomposition of the gradient is:
\begin{equation}
  \label{eq:polar}
  \partial_A\Phi = (u_0 + \sigma)\,n_A,
\end{equation}
where $\sigma(X)$ is the amplitude (radial) fluctuation and
$n_A(X)$ is the orientation collective variable with vacuum value
$\langle n_A\rangle = \delta_{Aw}$.

\noindent\textbf{Status of $n_A$.}
The orientation variable belongs to the vacuum level of description~(P4);
it is not a second fundamental microscopic degree of freedom
and does not appear in the fundamental action.


\subsection{Sector map: amplitude, soft orientation, and macroscopic
\texorpdfstring{$\phi$}{phi}-sector}
\label{sec:sector_map}

Three qualitatively distinct structures must be kept separate throughout
the rest of the theory.

\paragraph{Amplitude mode $\sigma$ (radial fluctuation).}
A fluctuation of $|\partial\Phi|$ around the uniform ordered background $u_0$.
\begin{itemize}
  \item Nature: UV fluctuation of the condensate amplitude.
  \item Mass scale: heavy, with characteristic radial scale set by the
    bare condensate potential ($m_\sigma^2\sim V''(\phi_0)$;
    cf.~\eqref{eq:sigma_mass}).
  \item Role: integrates out at $E\ll m_\sigma$; stabilises the ordered
    branch and sets the UV cutoff of the broken-phase EFT.
  \item It is not the Standard-Model Higgs boson.
\end{itemize}

\paragraph{Soft orientation sector.}
A broken-branch orientation sector can be introduced by writing
\begin{equation}
  Q_A \equiv \partial_A\Phi = u\,n_A,
  \qquad u = |Q|, \qquad n_A n^A = 1.
  \label{eq:QA_un_decomp}
\end{equation}
At the purely geometric level the ordered branch selects a preferred
direction and breaks $O(4)\to O(3)$, so that the vacuum manifold is
$O(4)/O(3)\simeq S^3$.
However, in the present scalar-only ECT basis $Q_A$ is not an arbitrary
vector field but an exact one-form, $Q_A=\partial_A\Phi$.
Therefore the orientation sector is subject to the integrability
constraint
\begin{equation}
  \partial_A Q_B - \partial_B Q_A = 0.
  \label{eq:integrability_main}
\end{equation}
This sharply reduces the naive Goldstone counting;
see Section~\ref{sec:goldstone_integrability_main} and
Appendix~\ref{app:goldstone_integrability}.

\paragraph{Macroscopic $\phi$-sector.}
Slow classical spatial variations of the ordered background on galactic
and cosmological scales.
\begin{itemize}
  \item Nature: classical infrared background variation rather than a
    UV quantum excitation.
  \item Characteristic scale: kpc--Gpc.
  \item Role: gives rise to $G_{\rm eff}(\mathbf{x})$ and to the
    macroscopic gravitational closure~(Section~\ref{sec:macroscopic}).
  \item Relation to $\sigma$: distinct---$\sigma$ is a UV fluctuation of
    the condensate amplitude, whereas the macroscopic $\phi$-sector is a
    slow classical variation of the ordered background itself.
\end{itemize}

\medskip
\noindent\textbf{Key consequence.}
The rotation-curve and RAR/BTFR success of ECT~(Section~\ref{sec:macroscopic})
uses only the macroscopic $\phi$-sector.
It does not rely on the Goldstone/soft-orientation discussion below.
Conversely, the soft orientation sector must not be used as a
dark-matter patch for phenomena already explained by the macroscopic
$\phi$-closure.

\subsection{Integrability and the status of the orientation sector}
\label{sec:goldstone_integrability_main}

The crucial point is that in the current ECT basis the ordered quantity
is $Q_A=\partial_A\Phi$, so the decomposition $Q_A=u\,n_A$ does
\emph{not} define independent fields $u$ and $n_A$.
Instead they must satisfy the exact integrability condition
\begin{equation}
  \partial_A(u n_B)-\partial_B(u n_A)=0.
  \label{eq:integrability_un}
\end{equation}

Linearising around the ordered background $Q_A^{(0)} = u_0\,\delta_{Aw}$
and writing
\begin{equation}
  u=u_0+\sigma, \qquad
  n_A=(\pi_i,\;1)+\mathcal{O}(\pi^2),
\end{equation}
one finds $\delta Q_i = u_0\,\pi_i$, $\delta Q_w = \sigma$.
Since $\delta Q_A$ must itself be a gradient, there exists a scalar
fluctuation $\chi$ such that $\delta Q_A = \partial_A\chi$, hence
\begin{equation}
  u_0\,\pi_i = \partial_i\chi, \qquad \sigma = \partial_w\chi.
  \label{eq:goldstone_reduction_main}
\end{equation}
Therefore the apparent orientation triplet is not an independent set of
three propagating microscopic modes: at the linear scalar-only level it
reduces to \emph{one longitudinal soft scalar degree of freedom~$\chi$}.
Equivalently, $\epsilon_{ijk}\,\partial_j\pi_k = 0$, so the
transverse/vortical part of the naive orientation sector is eliminated.

\paragraph{Interpretation: inverse-Higgs-type reduction.}
This is an inverse-Higgs-type
reduction~\cite{Ivanov1975,McArthur2010} characteristic of spacetime
symmetry breaking: the broken object $Q_A=\partial_A\Phi$ is a
geometric gradient rather than an internal multiplet.
One must therefore distinguish two statements:
\begin{enumerate}
  \item the ordered branch geometrically breaks $O(4)\to O(3)$ and
    admits a minimal $O(4)/O(3)\simeq S^3$ orientation description;
  \item in the strict scalar-only microscopic theory, the independent
    soft content surviving at the linear level is only
    \emph{one longitudinal scalar mode~$\chi$}.
\end{enumerate}
The full technical derivation is given in
Appendix~\ref{app:goldstone_integrability}.

\subsection{Minimal soft orientation EFT and phenomenological status}
\label{sec:goldstone_soft_eft}

Although the strict scalar-only microscopic content is reduced by
integrability, it remains useful to organise the broken branch by a
minimal orientation EFT.
In that effective description the vacuum manifold
$O(4)/O(3)\simeq S^3$ supports a three-component orientation map,
but this must be understood as an \emph{effective parametrisation},
not as a theorem about three independent physical Goldstone particles.

\paragraph{Quadratic soft-sector EFT.}
If the broken-phase EFT contains the local orientation-elastic term
$\frac{Z_n(u)}{2}u^2(\partial_B n_A)(\partial^B n^A)$,
then at uniform background $u=u_0$ the effective stiffness is
\begin{equation}
  \kappa_n = Z_n(u_0)\,u_0^2.
  \label{eq:kappa_n_main}
\end{equation}
This is a Level~B EFT identity.
The coefficient $Z_n$ is not yet derived from bare postulates P1--P5.

In the minimal EFT the surviving mode $\chi$ has a linear soft
dispersion relation
\begin{equation}
  \omega^2 = c_\chi^2\,k^2, \qquad
  c_\chi^2 = \frac{K_s}{K_w}\,c_*^2,
  \label{eq:chi_dispersion_main}
\end{equation}
where $K_w, K_s$ are EFT stiffness coefficients along $w$ and spatial
directions respectively.
The stronger statement $c_\chi=c_*$ requires $K_s=K_w$, which is not
yet established.

\paragraph{What remains true, and what does not.}
At the P1--P5 level there is no local $O(3)$ gauge structure, hence no
Higgs-type eating mechanism in the foundational
sector~\cite{GoldstoneSalamWeinberg1962}.
Any electroweak interpretation requires an additional scale
$v_2\sim246$~GeV and a second dynamical step~(OP-EW).

The soft orientation sector must \emph{not} be used as a dark-matter
replacement for macroscopic phenomena already explained by the
$\phi$-sector. At most, the broken phase contains a constrained soft
longitudinal mode whose phenomenological role remains open.

\subsection{EFT quadratic action for fluctuations}

\label{sec:eft_quadratic}

\textit{Status: EFT consequence (Theorem~\ref{thm:tensor_uniqueness}
applied to the broken-phase EFT).}

After integrating out the heavy radial mode ($E\ll m_\sigma$),
the effective quadratic action for soft fluctuation~$\varphi$
takes the form~\eqref{eq:quadratic_action} with the kinetic
tensor~\eqref{eq:K_general}. The EFT motivation for this form
via integrating out $\sigma$ is detailed in
Appendix~\ref{app:eft_derivation}.

\subsection{Mode hierarchy and the branching structure}
\label{sec:mode_hierarchy}

\begin{center}
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.4}
\begin{tabular}{p{3cm}p{3.5cm}p{5cm}}
\hline\hline
Mode & Scale & Role \\
\hline
Radial $\sigma$ & $E\sim m_\sigma$ & UV cutoff of EFT; integrates out for $E\ll m_\sigma$ \\
Orientation $\delta n_i$ & $E\ll m_\sigma$, soft & Geometric sector; in scalar-only ECT reduced by
integrability to one longitudinal scalar $\chi$~(Level~B; see
Section~\ref{sec:goldstone_integrability_main}) \\
Scalar $\varphi$ & $E\ll m_\sigma$ & Scalar excitations of Lorentzian sector \\
Phase $\theta$ & Topological sector & Coherent IR; pre-quantum action scale \\
\hline\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

\noindent\textbf{Spin-2 content of the geometric branch.}
\textit{Note on terminology:} ``spin-2'' is used here in the purely
classical group-theoretic sense---a rank-2 symmetric tensor under
the rotation group $O(3)$. This does not require quantisation and
involves no reference to~$\hbar$.

The three orientation modes $\delta n_i$ form a rank-2 symmetric tensorial
sector. Through the effective metric $g^{\rm eff}_{AB}(n_A)$,
small deformations $\delta n_i$ induce metric fluctuations $h_{\mu\nu}$.
With a massless dispersion $\omega^2=c_n^2 k^2$ at $\alpha=2\beta$,
the geometric branch contains a tensorial long-wavelength sector
compatible with a massless rank-2 description.

Furthermore, the existence of this massless tensorial sector and
its coupling to the conserved stress-energy tensor
(Appendix~\ref{app:noether_full}) make the geometric branch of ECT
compatible with a Deser-type route~\cite{Deser1970} to massless spin-2
gravity. On the ECT basics level this should be understood not as
a completed derivation of full nonlinear gravitational dynamics,
but as a structural motivation for why Macroscopic Physics should
develop precisely the spin-2 geometric branch.
The complete development---gauge reduction, two physical polarisations,
linearised and nonlinear gravitational equations---belongs to
Section~\ref{sec:macroscopic}.

\subsection{Status of results in this section}
\label{sec:spectrum_status}

\begin{center}
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.4}
\begin{tabular}{p{5.5cm}p{3.5cm}p{4.0cm}}
\hline\hline
Result & Status & Basis \\
\hline
Polar decomposition $\partial_A\Phi=(u_0+\sigma)n_A$
  & Level~A & P4 + Theorem~\ref{thm:tensor_uniqueness} \\
Quadratic EFT action~\eqref{eq:quadratic_action}
  & Level~A (EFT) & Uniqueness of $K^{AB}$ \\
Equation of motion~\eqref{eq:eom_broken}
  & Level~A (EFT) & Variation of~\eqref{eq:quadratic_action} \\
Lorentzian dispersion~\eqref{eq:dispersion} for $\alpha>\beta$
  & Level~A (EFT) & Theorem~\ref{thm:tensor_uniqueness} + $\alpha>\beta$ \\
Klein--Gordon equation~\eqref{eq:KG}
  & Level~A (EFT) & Interpretational step $w\to -ic_*t$ \\
Free Hamiltonian $H\geq 0$~\eqref{eq:H_free}
  & Level~A (quadratic) & Canonical normalisation; no tachyon/ghost \\
Identification of $m_{\rm eff}^2$ with $V''(\phi_0)$
  & Open (OP-grad) & Requires explicit EFT matching \\
Integrability of $Q_A=\partial_A\Phi$
  & Level~A & Scalar-only basis of P3 \\
Linear reduction $\pi_i=\partial_i\chi/u_0$, $\sigma=\partial_w\chi$
  & Level~A & Integrability around the ordered background \\
Minimal 3-component orientation EFT on $O(4)/O(3)\simeq S^3$
  & Level~B (effective description) & Broken-branch parametrisation only \\
Independent soft mode in scalar-only broken phase: one longitudinal scalar $\chi$
  & Level~B & Inverse-Higgs-type reduction~\cite{Ivanov1975,McArthur2010} \\
Orientation-sector stiffness $\kappa_n = Z_n(u_0)\,u_0^2$
  & Level~B (EFT) & Ordered-branch EFT identity \\
Derivation of $Z_n$ from bare P1--P5
  & Open & EFT matching not completed \\
Linear soft dispersion $\omega^2=c_\chi^2 k^2$
  & Level~B (EFT) & Minimal reduced EFT \\
Equality $c_\chi=c_*$
  & Open & Requires $K_s=K_w$; not yet derived \\
Three independent physical Goldstone particles
  & Not established & Reduced by integrability in scalar-only basis \\
No Higgs-type eating at the P1--P5 level
  & Level~B (structural) & No local $O(3)$ gauge structure \\
Pseudo-Goldstone mass mechanism
  & Open (OP-GM) & Requires explicit breaking source beyond P1--P5 \\
Relation to electroweak gauge/Higgs sector
  & Open (OP-EW) & Requires second condensate scale and localisation \\
Variational selection of realised $\Phi$-configurations
  & Level~A & DP (basic definition of the theory) \\
Emergent extremal dynamics in reduced effective sectors
  & Level~B & Inherited from variational selection after branch reduction \\
Action scale $S_0$ as ECT precursor of $\hbar$
  & Level~B & Phase periodicity + coherent winding sectors~(BR1) \\
Strict identification $S_0=\hbar$
  & Open & Requires full quantum-sector reconstruction \\
\hline\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

\medskip
\noindent\textit{What is intentionally not done here.}
This section does not introduce $\hbar$, canonical commutation relations,
or the Born rule. Only the result that broken-phase excitations satisfy
a Lorentzian wave equation with relativistic dispersion is obtained.
The quantum regime is developed in Section~\ref{sec:quantum}
after deriving the corresponding formalism from the condensate structure.


% ============================================================
% SECTION 5: FUNDAMENTAL SCALES, MATCHING RELATIONS, CONSTANTS
% ============================================================
\section{Fundamental Scales, Matching Relations, and the Status of Constants}
\label{sec:constants}

After establishing the postulates and the broken-phase dynamics,
we systematically distinguish: which parameters are microscopic,
which are effective, which are fixed by observational matching,
and which are not yet derived.


\subsection{Three condensate scales: structure, hierarchy, and open problems}
\label{sec:three_scales}

ECT contains three qualitatively different scales: a UV stiffness
scale, a low-energy matching scale, and an emergent IR variation scale.
They are \emph{not} three independent VEVs of three different fields.

\begin{center}
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.3}
\begin{tabular}{p{1.6cm}p{2.8cm}p{3.5cm}p{2.4cm}p{2.3cm}}
\hline
Scale & Value & Physical origin in ECT & Status
  & Observable anchor \\
\hline
$u_0$ & ${\sim}\,\bar{M}_{\rm Pl}\approx2.44\times10^{18}$\,GeV
  & UV gradient-condensate amplitude; gravitational stiffness scale
  & Level~B (matching)
  & $G_N$ \\
$v_2$ & $\approx246.22$\,GeV
  & Low-energy matching to electroweak EFT; structural candidate
    for second transition
  & Constraint / Level~B
  & $G_F$ \\
$v_{\rm gal}$ & kpc-scale variation length
  & Emergent IR scale of ordered-branch amplitude variation;
    $\phi$-sector
  & Level~B (structural)
  & SPARC/RAR \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

These three scales are not of the same conceptual type.
The UV scale $u_0$ is associated with the gradient condensate and the
stiffness of the ordered branch.
The intermediate scale $v_2$ is, in the present formulation, a
low-energy matching constraint if a second transition exists.
The galactic scale is an emergent IR variation scale of the
$\phi$-sector closure, not a second microscopic condensate VEV.

The multiplicity~S6 and topological completeness~S9 of the
$\Phi$-medium make a multi-scale structure structurally admissible,
but do not by themselves explain the specific hierarchy between
these scales.

\begin{figure}[ht]
\centering
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{figures/fig_condensate_scales.png}
\caption{Illustrative schematic of the three condensate scale hierarchy
in ECT. \emph{Left:} running coupling $\lambda(\mu)$ as a function of
scale $\mu$ --- shown as a schematic estimate only; the actual RG
running in ECT has not yet been derived from first principles~(open;
OP-EW). \emph{Right:} the three characteristic scales $u_0\sim\bar{M}_{\rm Pl}$,
$v_2\sim246$\,GeV, and the galactic IR scale, separated by large
logarithms. The intermediate scale $v_2$ is fixed by electroweak
low-energy matching~\eqref{eq:v2_GF}, not derived from the condensate
action. The figure is a structural illustration of scale separation and possible
RG logic, not a quantitative ECT derivation of the intermediate scale.}
\label{fig:three_condensate_scales}
\end{figure}

\paragraph{High scale: $u_0\sim\bar{M}_{\rm Pl}$.}
The gradient condensate amplitude $u_0$ sets the UV stiffness
of the ordered branch.
In the geometric sector of ECT, orientation modes $\delta n_i$
acquire a kinetic term with effective stiffness
$\kappa_n = Z_n(u_0)\,u_0^2$~(Section~\ref{sec:goldstone_soft_eft}).
Matching to linearised gravity~(Section~\ref{sec:GN_matching}):
\begin{equation}
  M_G^2 \sim Z_n(u_0)\,u_0^2,
  \label{eq:MG_u0}
\end{equation}
with $M_G\equiv\bar{M}_{\rm Pl}=(8\pi G_N)^{-1/2}
\approx2.44\times10^{18}$\,GeV.
Under the natural assumption $Z_n\sim1$:
\begin{equation}
  \boxed{u_0 \;\sim\; \bar{M}_{\rm Pl}}
  \qquad\text{(Level~B: requires }Z_n\sim1\text{; pending OP2).}
  \label{eq:u0_Mpl}
\end{equation}
A sub-Planckian $u_0$ would require an anomalously large $Z_n$
suppression; a trans-Planckian $u_0$ would conflict with EFT
validity. The identification $u_0\sim\bar{M}_{\rm Pl}$ is
therefore the unique natural matching.

\paragraph{The gradient condensate and the open problem OP-grad.}
Postulate~P4 introduces $u_0$ as the gradient condensate amplitude
$\langle\partial_A\Phi\rangle = u_0\,\delta_{Aw}$, independently
of the bare potential minimum
$\phi_0 = \sqrt{\mu^2/\lambda}$~(P3).
This independence is deliberate: the ordered vacuum of ECT is
characterised by the gradient structure, not by the field value.
Nevertheless, a dimensionally natural estimate is obtained by
combining the bare-amplitude scale
$\phi_0\sim\mu/\sqrt{\lambda}$ with the radial correlation length
$\xi_{\rm cond}\sim m_\varphi^{-1}$, where
$m_\varphi\sim\sqrt{2}\,\mu$.
This gives
\begin{equation}
  u_0 \;\sim\; \frac{\phi_0}{\xi_{\rm cond}}
  \;\sim\; \phi_0\,m_\varphi
  \;\sim\; \frac{\mu}{\sqrt{\lambda}}\cdot\sqrt{2}\,\mu
  \;\sim\; \frac{\mu^2}{\sqrt{\lambda}}.
  \label{eq:u0_dimensional}
\end{equation}
This is only a dimensional self-consistency estimate, not a
derivation.
The explicit dynamical mechanism by which the gradient condensate
emerges from the bare action remains an open foundational
problem~(OP-grad).
Until OP-grad is closed, $u_0$ should be treated as an
independent Level~2 parameter~(Section~\ref{sec:param_hierarchy}).

\paragraph{Galactic IR scale: emergent branch-variation scale.}
The galactic-scale physics in ECT does not arise from a separate
microscopic VEV.
It is the scale at which slow spatial variations
$u_0\to u_0(\mathbf{x})$ of the ordered-branch amplitude become
dynamically important in the $\phi$-sector~(Section~\ref{sec:sector_map}).
The characteristic galactic radius is
\begin{equation}
  r_0 \sim \left(\frac{G_N M_\star}{g^\dagger}\right)^{1/2},
  \label{eq:r0_gal}
\end{equation}
where $g^\dagger\approx1.2\times10^{-10}$\,m\,s$^{-2}$ is
the RAR acceleration scale~\cite{McGaugh2016}.
For $M_\star\sim10^{10}\,M_\odot$ this gives $r_0\sim5$--$20$\,kpc
(Level~B; Section~\ref{sec:mp_galactic}).
\begin{equation}
  \boxed{v_{\rm gal} \text{ is an emergent IR scale, not a second UV condensate VEV.}}
\end{equation}

\paragraph{Why $g^\dagger\sim cH_0$.}
The structural proximity $g^\dagger\sim cH_0/(2\pi)\approx
1.04\times10^{-10}$\,m\,s$^{-2}$ is one of the most suggestive
numerical coincidences in ECT.
At present, ECT does not derive this relation from first principles.
Rather, it indicates that the galactic transition scale may be tied to
the cosmological background scale of the ordered branch.
A full derivation of $g^\dagger$ from condensate dynamics remains
open~(OP-galactic; Section~\ref{sec:mp_galactic}).

\paragraph{Intermediate scale $v_2$ and the electroweak matching.}
If the ECT condensate undergoes a second ordering step, the
intermediate scale is fully fixed by low-energy matching:
\begin{equation}
  v_2 = \left(\sqrt{2}\,G_F\right)^{-1/2}
  \approx 246.22\,\text{GeV},
  \label{eq:v2_GF}
\end{equation}
where $G_F=1.1664\times10^{-5}$\,GeV$^{-2}$
is the measured Fermi constant~\cite{ParticleDataGroup2022}.
This is not a free parameter: any deviation would give wrong
$W$ and $Z$ masses. A structural candidate mechanism for the
second transition $O(3)\to O(2)$ is discussed in
Appendix~\ref{app:second_transition}; it remains an open
problem of ECT.

\paragraph{Hierarchy and three distinct open problems.}
The three scales obey
\begin{equation}
  u_0 \sim \bar{M}_{\rm Pl} \gg v_2 \gg v_{\rm gal},
  \qquad
  \frac{v_2}{u_0} \approx 10^{-16}.
  \label{eq:hierarchy}
\end{equation}

\paragraph{The hierarchy problem in the ECT context.}
The ratio $v_2/u_0\approx 10^{-16}$ is the ECT counterpart of the
standard gauge hierarchy problem.
ECT does not solve this problem in the current formulation.
However, the ECT setting differs from the Standard Model in one
structurally important respect: $v_2$ is not a fundamental vacuum
expectation value of a second independent field, but the matching
scale of a \emph{second ordering step} within the same
condensate~(Appendix~\ref{app:second_transition}).
If this second transition has a dynamical origin---for instance
through RG running of the effective orientation coupling from
$u_0$ down to $v_2$ (Fig.~\ref{fig:three_condensate_scales})---the
large hierarchy could in principle be accommodated logarithmically
rather than by fine-tuning.
This is a structural possibility, not a derived result:
the explicit RG calculation within ECT has not been performed and
remains part of OP-EW.
Any claim that ECT resolves the hierarchy problem would be
premature.

Since $v_2$ and $v_{\rm gal}$ are qualitatively different
objects, the three-scale structure contains three distinct open
problems:
\begin{description}
  \item[OP-grad (gradient condensate).]
    What is the dynamical mechanism by which the gradient
    condensate $\langle\partial_A\Phi\rangle = u_0\,\delta_{Aw}$
    emerges from the bare action P3?
    Dimensional analysis~\eqref{eq:u0_dimensional} is consistent,
    but a first-principles derivation remains open.
  \item[OP-EW (electroweak hierarchy).]
    Why does a second condensate scale arise at $v_2\ll u_0$?
    A structural candidate mechanism is described in
    Appendix~\ref{app:second_transition}; its realisation
    within ECT remains open.
  \item[OP-galactic (IR closure).]
    What determines the precise value of $g^\dagger$ in
    terms of $u_0$ and cosmological parameters?
    Structurally $g^\dagger\sim cH_0/(2\pi)$ at the level of a suggestive
    order-of-magnitude coincidence~(Level~B), but a
    first-principles derivation from the condensate
    functional is not yet available.
\end{description}

\paragraph{Consistency between gravitational and quantum scales.}
A distinctive structural feature of ECT is that the gravitational
sector and the pre-quantum action scale~$S_0^{\rm EFT}$ both arise
from the same ordered medium.
At the current stage, however, their explicit parametrisations are not
yet fully reduced to an identical set of microscopic variables, because
the relation between the gradient condensate scale $u_0$ and the bare
potential data remains open~(OP-grad).
The relevant matching relations are:
\begin{equation}
  G_N = \frac{c_*^4}{8\pi M_G^2},
  \qquad
  S_0^{\rm EFT} = \frac{K_\theta\,m_\varphi}{2},
  \label{eq:GN_S0_common}
\end{equation}
where $M_G$, $K_\theta\sim v_0^2$, $m_\varphi=\sqrt{2\lambda}\,v_0$,
and $c_*$ are all functions of the condensate data
(Section~\ref{sec:hbar_status}).
Neither $G_N$ nor $S_0^{\rm EFT}$ is derived from the other; both are
Level~B matching relations.
But the fact that they share a common parametric origin means that
ECT therefore suggests a structural consistency relation between the
gravitational and quantum sectors: both originate in the same ordered
condensate, even though their explicit matching relations are not yet
fully unified at the microscopic level.
This is the structural content of the common origin of the
gravitational and quantum scales in ECT: not that one is derived from
the other, but that both emerge from a single ordered medium and their
eventual relation is expected to be constrained by the condensate
structure once OP-grad is resolved.

\noindent\textbf{Status summary:}
\begin{center}
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.2}
\begin{tabular}{p{5.8cm}p{2.0cm}p{4.0cm}}
\hline
Statement & Status & Comment \\
\hline
Existence of UV scale $u_0$ from P4 & A & \\
$u_0\sim\bar{M}_{\rm Pl}$ & B & Requires $Z_n\sim1$; OP2 \\
$u_0\sim\phi_0\cdot m_\varphi$: dimensional
  self-consistency & B & Eq.~\eqref{eq:u0_dimensional};
  OP-grad \\
$v_2=(\sqrt{2}G_F)^{-1/2}$ as IR matching constraint & Constraint & If second transition exists \\
Galactic scale is emergent IR branch variation & B & Via $\phi$-sector and RAR \\
$g^\dagger\sim cH_0/(2\pi)$: suggestive structural coincidence & B &
  OP-galactic \\
$G_N$ and $S_0^{\rm EFT}$ are controlled by the same
  condensate parameters & B & Eq.~\eqref{eq:GN_S0_common};
  structural link \\
$\mathfrak{o}(3)\cong\mathfrak{su}(2)$: structural compatibility
  with EW-like second transition & A & Algebra only \\
Second transition $O(3)\to O(2)$ as structural candidate & B & Requires phase--orientation coupling \\
Gradient condensate from bare action & Open (OP-grad) & \\
Phase--orientation coupling as dynamical driver & Open (OP-EW) & \\
First-principles derivation of $v_2$ & Open (OP-EW) & \\
First-principles derivation of $g^\dagger$ & Open (OP-galactic) & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

\subsection{Parameter hierarchy by level of description}
\label{sec:param_hierarchy}

\paragraph{Level 1. Bare micro-parameters~(P3).}
The potential $V(\Phi)$ contains two parameters:
$\mu^2>0$, $\lambda>0$, with bare radial stiffness:
\begin{equation}
  m_\sigma^2 = V''(\phi_0) = 2\mu^2,\qquad
  \phi_0 = \sqrt{\mu^2/\lambda}.
  \label{eq:sigma_mass}
\end{equation}
These characterise the microscopic field dynamics only and
do not directly fix the ordered vacuum.

\paragraph{Level 2. Vacuum-sector parameters~(P4).}
Postulate~P4 introduces $u_0>0$, the gradient condensate amplitude
$\langle\partial_A\Phi\rangle = u_0\,\delta_{Aw}$.
This is an independent parameter; its relation to $\phi_0$
and $\lambda$ from the bare theory requires additional
matching (OP-grad).

\paragraph{Level 3. Broken-phase EFT parameters.}
The effective kinetic tensor contains $\alpha>\beta>0$ and $m_{\rm eff}^2$,
where $m_{\rm eff}^2\neq m_\sigma^2$ in general.

\paragraph{Level 4. Coherent-sector parameters~(BR1).}
Within the smooth coherent branch~(BR1), the pre-quantum topological
action scale $S_0$ is introduced via the elementary-loop
action $S_{\rm loop,min}=2\pi S_0$~(Section~\ref{sec:hbar_status}).
The identification $S_0=\hbar$ is deferred to
Section~\ref{sec:quantum}.

\subsection{Effective propagation speed $c_*$}
\label{sec:c_star}

\textit{Status: Level~A in broken-phase EFT.}

From the dispersion relation~\eqref{eq:dispersion}:
\begin{equation}
  \label{eq:c_star_def}
  \boxed{c_*^2 = \frac{\beta}{\alpha-\beta}.}
\end{equation}
This is a strict EFT consequence; it requires neither quantum
formalism nor a gravitational sector.

\paragraph{Observational matching.}
The constraint that gravitational and electromagnetic signals propagate
at the same speed~\cite{LIGO_Virgo_GW170817_2017} motivates the
benchmark $c_*=c$. In canonical units ($\beta=1$) this corresponds
to $\alpha=2\beta=2$.
The parameters $(\alpha,\beta)$ are EFT parameters not derivable
from the bare micro-action P3 in the current formulation.

\subsection{Gravitational stiffness scale and Newton's constant}
\label{sec:GN_matching}

\textit{Status: Level~B --- geometric EFT sector; $G_N$ fixed by matching.}

In the geometric sector of ECT the orientation modes $\delta n_i$
produce metric fluctuations. At the quadratic level this leads to
a Fierz--Pauli-type~\cite{Fierz1939} action for spin-2 excitations
with stiffness coefficient $M_G^2$:
\begin{equation}
  S_{\rm geom}^{(2)}\sim M_G^2\int d^4x\,\partial_\lambda h_{\mu\nu}\,\partial^\lambda h^{\mu\nu}+\ldots
\end{equation}

\paragraph{Matching to Newton's constant.}
Comparison with linearised GR (Einstein--Hilbert action in the linear limit):
\begin{equation}
  \label{eq:GN_matching}
  G_N = \frac{c_*^4}{8\pi M_G^2}.
\end{equation}
In natural units $c=\hbar=1$ (used as a working convention
independently of the final status of~$\hbar$):
\begin{equation}
  M_G \equiv \bar{M}_{\rm Pl} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{8\pi G_N}}
  \approx 2.435\times 10^{18}\,{\rm GeV}.
\end{equation}
This is a numerical EFT matching, not a first-principles derivation.
The numerical value in GeV requires $\hbar$ and $c$ for unit conversion;
strict justification follows from Section~\ref{sec:quantum}.

The relation~\eqref{eq:GN_matching} is geometric EFT matching,
not a derivation of $G_N$ from the bare micro-action~P3.
How $M_G$ relates to $(u_0,\alpha,\beta)$ remains an open
problem~(OP2--OP3). This is developed in Section~\ref{sec:macroscopic}.

\subsection{Radial-mode scales and the correlation length}
\label{sec:sigma_scales}

From the bare potential P3 the EFT matching-scale estimate:
\begin{equation}
  \xi_{\rm cond}\sim m_\sigma^{-1} = (2\mu^2)^{-1/2}.
\end{equation}
This is supported by the exponential decay of the Euclidean propagator
of the heavy radial mode (Appendix~\ref{app:corr_length}):
$G_\sigma(r)\sim e^{-m_\sigma r}/r^{3/2}$ for $r\to\infty$.

A natural phenomenological matching hypothesis is $\xi_{\rm cond}\sim\ell_{\rm Pl}$,
where $\ell_{\rm Pl}=\sqrt{G_N\hbar/c^3}\approx 1.6\times 10^{-35}$\,m.
This should be understood as a motivated matching hypothesis,
not a strict derivation: $\ell_{\rm Pl}$ depends on~$\hbar$,
which is not yet derived at this stage.

\subsection{Action scale $S_0$ as the ECT precursor of $\hbar$}
\label{sec:hbar_status}

\textit{Status: three-layered.
Layer~1 (strict within the reduced $U(1)$-extended phase-loop EFT):
the winding-sector action admits a sharp lower bound at fixed loop
length, with the minimum achieved by uniform winding.
Layer~2 (Level~B EFT input): specifying the characteristic size of an
elementary coherent loop yields a distinguished EFT action scale
$S_0^{\rm EFT}$.
Layer~3 (Level~B matching): the identification
$S_0^{\rm EFT}\leftrightarrow\hbar$ is the strongest structural bridge
to quantum mechanics, but remains a matching statement, not a
parameter-free derivation.}

A central question for ECT is whether quantum mechanics requires the
external introduction of Planck's constant, or whether a distinguished
action scale already emerges within the coherent-topological branch of
the condensate.
The present framework supports the second interpretation:
before identifying $\hbar$ itself, ECT already contains a natural
\emph{pre-quantum action scale} $S_0$.
What follows clarifies why $S_0$ arises, what it means for the
classical--quantum divide, and where the boundary between established
and open results lies.

ECT does not insert $S_0$ by hand to imitate quantum mechanics.
It is the action scale associated with the elementary
coherent-topological cycle of the condensate, forced by phase
single-valuedness within the $U(1)$-extended phase EFT.

% ============ LAYER 1 ============

\paragraph{Layer~1: compact phase and the variational bound
(strict within the reduced $U(1)$-extended phase-loop EFT).}

\emph{Coherent phase variable.}
In the smooth coherent branch~(BR1), the collective state is
characterised not only by an amplitude $\rho$ but also by a
phase-like variable~$\theta$.
Because the coherent state is physically single-valued, this phase
is defined only modulo a full winding:
\begin{equation}
  \theta \sim \theta + 2\pi.
  \label{eq:theta_periodicity}
\end{equation}
It should be noted that the real scalar field $\Phi$ of P3 does not
by itself carry a compact $U(1)$ phase variable.
The extension to $\Phi_{\rm eff}=\rho\,e^{i\theta}$ is an additional
modelling step for the coherent charged sector.
Accordingly, the periodicity $\theta\sim\theta+2\pi$ and the
associated winding number are strict statements within the
$U(1)$-extended phase EFT, not direct topological consequences of
the vacuum manifold $O(4)/O(3)\simeq S^3$
(which has $\pi_1(S^3)=0$).

\emph{Phase action.}
At wavelengths large compared to the radial-core scale, and within
the smooth coherent sector, the radial mode is approximately frozen
and the effective dynamics is dominated by the phase.
We write the reduced phase action in the schematic EFT form
\begin{equation}
  S_\theta[\theta]
  = \frac{K_\theta}{2}
    \int d^4X\;(\partial_A\theta)^2,
  \label{eq:S_theta_layer1}
\end{equation}
with $K_\theta\sim v_0^2$ in the simplest realisation.

\emph{Sharp lower bound on loop action
(Cauchy--Schwarz inequality).}
Consider a closed coherent loop $\gamma$ of proper length $L$
in the phase EFT with winding number
$n[\gamma]=\frac{1}{2\pi}\oint_\gamma\partial_\ell\theta\,d\ell$.
The reduced 1D action along the loop is
\begin{equation}
  S_{\rm loop}[\theta]
  = \frac{K_\theta}{2}\oint_\gamma d\ell\;
    (\partial_\ell\theta)^2.
  \label{eq:S_loop_def}
\end{equation}
By the Cauchy--Schwarz inequality applied to the constant function
$f=1$ and $g=\partial_\ell\theta$:
\begin{equation}
  \oint d\ell\;(\partial_\ell\theta)^2
  \;\geq\;
  \frac{1}{L}\left(\oint d\ell\;\partial_\ell\theta\right)^{\!2}
  = \frac{(2\pi n)^2}{L},
  \label{eq:CS_inequality}
\end{equation}
with equality if and only if $\partial_\ell\theta=\text{const}$
along the loop (uniform winding).
Therefore:
\begin{equation}
  \boxed{S_{\rm loop}[\theta]
  \;\geq\;
  \frac{2\pi^2 K_\theta\,n^2}{L},
  \qquad
  \text{equality at uniform winding.}}
  \label{eq:loop_bound}
\end{equation}
This is a strict variational result within the phase EFT:
the minimiser of loop action at fixed length and winding is the
uniform-gradient configuration, and the minimum scales as
$n^2/L$.

\emph{Important structural remark.}
Equation~\eqref{eq:loop_bound} provides a sharp lower bound for
each fixed $L$, but because the bound falls as $1/L$,
$S_{\rm loop}\to 0$ as $L\to\infty$.
The pure phase topology alone does \emph{not} yield a nonzero
global minimum of the loop action; additional physical input is
needed to obtain a distinguished action scale.

% ============ LAYER 2 ============

\paragraph{Layer~2: elementary-loop scale and the distinguished
action parameter (Level~B).}

\emph{Why the fixed-$L$ bound is not yet a nonzero global minimum.}
Equation~\eqref{eq:loop_bound} gives the minimum action in a
fixed length-and-winding sector, but because the bound falls as
$1/L$, it does not define a nonzero global minimum when the loop
length is allowed to vary.

\emph{Elementary coherent loop size.}
To obtain a distinguished EFT action scale, one must specify the
characteristic size of an elementary coherent loop.
The natural choice is the radial-core scale: for loops much larger
than $\xi_{\rm core}\sim m_\varphi^{-1}$, the smooth coherent
description breaks down and the loop can no longer be treated as a
single elementary coherent object within the reduced phase EFT.
The characteristic elementary loop length is therefore
\begin{equation}
  L_{\rm core}\sim 2\pi\,\xi_{\rm core}
  = \frac{2\pi}{m_\varphi}.
  \label{eq:Lcore}
\end{equation}
This is a Level~B EFT input: it uses the radial-mode correlation
length from the broken-phase potential, not topology alone.

\emph{Distinguished EFT action scale.}
Evaluating the fixed-$L$ lower bound~\eqref{eq:loop_bound} at the
elementary-loop size with $n=1$ gives the characteristic
elementary-loop action
\begin{equation}
  S_{\rm elem}
  = \frac{2\pi^2 K_\theta}{L_{\rm core}}
  = \pi\,K_\theta\,m_\varphi.
  \label{eq:S_elem}
\end{equation}
We then define the associated EFT action scale by
\begin{equation}
  \boxed{S_0^{\rm EFT}
  \;\equiv\;
  \frac{S_{\rm elem}}{2\pi}
  = \frac{K_\theta\,m_\varphi}{2}.}
  \label{eq:S0_EFT}
\end{equation}
This is a distinguished EFT scale associated with the elementary
coherent loop, not the global variational minimum over arbitrary
loop lengths~(Level~B).

\emph{Notational remark.}
Throughout the remainder of the paper the notation
$S_{\rm loop,min}=2\pi S_0$ may occasionally appear in earlier
sections.
In the present revised formulation, however, this quantity should be
understood as the elementary-loop action at the characteristic core
scale, not as a global variational minimum over arbitrary loop lengths.
It should be understood as the elementary-loop action
$S_{\rm elem}$ evaluated at the characteristic core scale
$L_{\rm core}$, not as a global variational minimum over
arbitrary loop lengths.

\emph{Self-consistency, not prediction.}
The relation~\eqref{eq:S0_EFT} should be read as a
self-consistency condition: if $S_0^{\rm EFT}$ is to be identified with
$\hbar$, then the condensate parameters must satisfy
$K_\theta\,m_\varphi/2\approx\hbar$.
This is analogous to the BCS gap equation, where the
superconducting gap $\Delta$ is determined self-consistently
rather than predicted from first principles.
The content of ECT is not that $\hbar$ is computed from nothing,
but that the EFT action scale identified with $\hbar$ and the
gravitational coupling $G_N$ both arise from the same ordered medium.
At the current stage, however, their explicit parametrisations are not
yet fully reduced to an identical set of microscopic variables, because
the relation between the gradient condensate scale $u_0$ and the bare
potential data remains open~(OP-grad).
The relevant matching relations are:
\begin{equation}
  S_0^{\rm EFT} = \frac{K_\theta\,m_\varphi}{2},
  \qquad
  G_N = \frac{c_*^4}{8\pi\,M_G^2},
  \label{eq:hbar_GN_same_condensate}
\end{equation}
where $M_G$, $K_\theta$, $m_\varphi$, and $c_*$ are all functions
of $(v_0,\lambda,\alpha,\beta)$.
ECT therefore suggests a structural consistency relation between the
gravitational and quantum sectors: both originate in the same ordered
condensate, even though their explicit matching relations are not yet
fully unified at the microscopic level.

% ============ LAYER 3 ============

\paragraph{Layer~3: identification with $\hbar$ (Level~B matching).}

In ordinary quantum theory, $\hbar$ is the quantity that renders the
action dimensionless in the phase factor
$\mathcal{A}\sim e^{iS/\hbar}$.
ECT reproduces the same structural role through $S_0^{\rm EFT}$:
\begin{equation}
  S_0^{\rm EFT} \;\text{ is the ECT precursor of }\; \hbar.
  \label{eq:S0_precursor_hbar}
\end{equation}
The working identification is
\begin{equation}
  \hbar \;\leftrightarrow\; S_0^{\rm EFT}
  \label{eq:hbar_S0_identification}
\end{equation}
(Level~B: strongly motivated structural identification, not yet a
strict theorem).

\emph{Why this is not yet Level~A.}
To promote the identification to Level~A, one would need to
derive from ECT not just a number with units of action, but the
full structural role of $\hbar$:
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)]
  \item a universal phase weighting $\mathcal{A}\sim e^{iS/S_0^{\rm EFT}}$
    for arbitrary histories, not only for special loops;
  \item a composition law for coherent amplitudes;
  \item canonical commutators normalised by $S_0^{\rm EFT}$;
  \item ultimately, reconstruction of the Hilbert-space structure
    and the Born rule.
\end{enumerate}
This is the programme of the Quantum
Sector~(Section~\ref{sec:quantum}), not a one-line identification.
Within that programme, partial results are already
available~(Sections~\ref{sec:qs_phase_dynamics}--\ref{sec:qs_ccr}),
but the full reconstruction is not yet complete.

\paragraph{Relation to the classical limit and variational dynamics.}
The emergence of $S_0^{\rm EFT}$ is fully compatible with the variational
principle.
At the fundamental level, realised configurations remain extremals
of $S_E[\Phi]$~(DP; Section~\ref{sec:noether}).
What $S_0^{\rm EFT}$ adds is a sensitivity scale for coherent phase relations:
when action differences satisfy $|\Delta S|\gg S_0^{\rm EFT}$, relative phase
oscillations become rapidly varying and an extremal, effectively
classical description dominates; when $|\Delta S|\sim S_0^{\rm EFT}$, phase
differences cannot be ignored and interference between coherent
histories becomes structurally relevant.
Thus $S_0^{\rm EFT}$ separates the extremal/classical regime from the
phase-sensitive regime, without contradicting the underlying
variational dynamics.

\paragraph{Coarse graining and emergent classicality.}
If branch histories are tracked at a resolution comparable to $S_0^{\rm EFT}$,
relative phases matter and quantum interference is expected.
If the description is coarse-grained over many branch sectors---for
instance when the environment has many degrees of
freedom~($N_{\rm eff}\gg1$) as in
Section~\ref{sec:arrow_of_time}---those relative phases are
effectively washed out.
ECT interprets classicality not as the absence of $S_0^{\rm EFT}$, but as the
loss of access to $S_0^{\rm EFT}$-sensitive phase information through coarse
graining.

\paragraph{Path-integral hint.}
The most natural amplitude assignment suggested by the
coherent-topological branch structure is
\begin{equation}
  \mathcal{A} \sim e^{iS/S_0^{\rm EFT}}.
  \label{eq:path_integral_hint}
\end{equation}
Equation~\eqref{eq:path_integral_hint} should be read as a strong
ECT motivation for the path-integral phase, not yet as a complete
derivation of the quantum measure or of the Born rule.

\paragraph{Comparison with familiar quantisation ideas.}

\emph{Bohr--Sommerfeld
quantisation~\cite{LandauLifshitzQM}:}
in semiclassical mechanics one has
$\oint p\,dq = 2\pi n\hbar$.
ECT leads instead to $\Delta S = 2\pi n S_0^{\rm EFT}$, but the origin is
coherent-topological phase winding, not orbital quantisation.

\emph{Flux quantisation and superfluid
winding~\cite{Volovik2003}:}
this is perhaps the closest analogy.
In superfluids, compact phase plus single-valuedness forces
quantised circulation.
ECT uses the same structural logic, applied to the action-phase
relation of the coherent branch rather than to a material
superfluid.

\emph{Path-integral phase
assignment~\cite{FeynmanHibbs1965}:}
standard quantum theory uses $e^{iS/\hbar}$ and takes $\hbar$ as
primitive.
ECT does not assume $\hbar$ from the outset; it explains why a
distinguished action scale should appear, and only then motivates
identifying that scale with $\hbar$.

\paragraph{Status summary.}

\begin{center}

  phase-loop EFT}} \\

  $U(1)$-extended phase EFT
  & B & Modelling extension of real $\Phi$ \\
Single-valuedness on closed loops
  & A & Topological consistency within that EFT \\

  & A & Integer winding number \\
Sharp lower bound on loop action at fixed length
  & A & Eq.~\eqref{eq:loop_bound}; Cauchy--Schwarz \\
