\paragraph{Terminological note: $v_0$ vs $u_0$.}
In sections following this foundational part (Quantum Dynamics,
Consistency Relations, and onwards), the older notation~$v_0$
is used for the condensate amplitude, corresponding to
$\langle\Phi\rangle=v_0$ in the original ECT formulation.
In the present foundational framework (Sections~\ref{sec:postulates}--\ref{sec:branching})
we use $u_0=|\langle\partial_A\Phi\rangle|$~(P4) for the
gradient condensate amplitude and $\phi_0=\sqrt{\mu^2/\lambda}$~(P3)
for the bare potential minimum.
The matching $u_0\leftrightarrow v_0$ is established via the
explicit condensate profile $\Phi_{\rm bg}=u_0 w+\Phi_*$
(Appendix~\ref{app:background_split}).
In the updated treatment of subsequent sections this distinction
will be made precise; for the present paper the two notations
coexist with this terminological note.



% ============================================================
% SECTION 6: BRANCHING STRUCTURE OF ECT
% ============================================================
\section{Branching Structure of ECT}
\label{sec:branching}\label{sec:roadmap}

The five working postulates P1--P5, together with the ordered
vacuum sector, the broken-phase spectrum, the scale hierarchy, and the
derived smooth coherent-sector rule~BR1, define a common condensate
framework from which two distinct but mutually consistent effective
branches emerge.

\subsection{Two classes of observables}
\label{sec:two_observables}

ECT contains a single fundamental field~$\Phi$~(P3).
Postulate~P4 introduces the ordered branch and, through the broken-phase
EFT analysis, the orientation variable~$n_A$ and the kinetic
tensor~\eqref{eq:K_general}, producing the geometric causal sector.
The derived smooth coherent-sector rule~BR1 introduces the collective
phase variable~$\theta$ with the winding condition
\eqref{eq:winding_condition}.

These two steps are structurally distinct and produce two qualitatively
different classes of observables:
\begin{enumerate}
  \item \textit{Phase-insensitive observables}: probe the coarse-grained
    geometric and stiffness response of the vacuum.
    Primary variables: $n_A$, $K^{AB}$, $g^{\rm eff}_{AB}$, $M_G$.
    These constitute the \emph{geometric branch}.
  \item \textit{Phase-sensitive observables}: probe coherence,
    winding structure, and non-trivial loop configurations.
    Primary variables: $\Phi_{\rm eff}$, $\theta$, winding numbers, $S_0$.
    These constitute the \emph{coherent-topological branch}.
\end{enumerate}

\subsection{The geometric branch (Macroscopic Physics)}
\label{sec:geom_branch}

Primary variables: $n_A$, $K^{AB}$, $g^{\rm eff}_{AB}$, $M_G$,
long-wavelength stiffness fields.

This branch contains in embryo:
effective Lorentzian geometry; propagation speed $c_*$~(Level~A);
gravitational stiffness~$M_G$ matched to $G_N$~(Level~B);
the long-wavelength sector from which gravity, cosmology, and
astrophysical phenomenology are developed.

\subsection{The coherent-topological branch (Quantum Sector)}
\label{sec:coh_branch}

Primary variables: $\Phi_{\rm eff}$, $\theta$,
winding condition~\eqref{eq:winding_condition}, $S_0$.

This branch will later develop:
identification of $S_0$ with observable~$\hbar$;
canonical quantisation relations;
connection between phase single-valuedness, loop quantisation,
and wave dynamics;
decoherence, probabilistic interpretation, and the quantum limit of ECT.

\subsection{Why branching is necessary}
\label{sec:why_branching}

The branching is not a matter of exposition but a structural
consequence of the condensate framework:
\begin{enumerate}
  \item P4 introduces the ordered branch and thereby the orientation field---this opens the geometric branch.
  \item BR1 identifies the smooth coherent phase sector with integer winding---this opens the coherent-topological branch.
  \item Both branches arise as different coarse-grainings of the same ordered condensate.
  \item Neither branch reduces to the other without loss of physical content.
\end{enumerate}
\textbf{Criterion.}
In ECT the effective branch is selected not by adding new postulates
but by the class of observables retained under coarse-graining.

\subsection{Variational principle and Noether structure}
\label{sec:noether}

\textit{Status: layered.
Physical configuration selection via extremality is Level~A (DP).
Noether structure from action symmetries is Level~A.
Inheritance by the ordered branches is Level~A/B.
Compatibility with the arrow of time and with the classical/quantum
divide is Level~B.
Full derivation of all effective branch functionals and late-time
charge reconstruction remains open.}

The variational principle in ECT is more than a formal device for
writing field equations.
Within the ontology of the $\Phi$-medium, it is the mathematical
criterion that selects \emph{physically realised configurations} from
the much larger set of ontologically admissible ones.
Extremality is therefore the bridge from the ontological layer of the
theory to its dynamical content.
The action principle belongs to the same foundational \emph{dynamical}
layer as DP, not to the ontological layer of properties S1--S9.

\paragraph{From admissibility to realisation.}
The structural properties S1--S9 of the $\Phi$-medium specify what
kinds of configurations are admissible: regular or defect-adjacent,
homogeneous or inhomogeneous, coherent or topologically nontrivial.
The dynamical principle~DP then states that physically realised
configurations are extremals of a local action functional compatible
with the basic symmetries of the medium:
\begin{equation}
  \label{eq:variational}
  \delta S_E[\Phi]\bigg|_{\rm on\text{-}shell} = 0.
\end{equation}
ECT thus separates two logically distinct notions:
\begin{itemize}
  \item \emph{admissibility} --- governed by the ontology of the medium;
  \item \emph{realisation} --- governed by variational selection.
\end{itemize}
This is why extremality is not merely a calculational convention in ECT:
it is the criterion by which physical histories are selected from the
space of possible configurations.

\paragraph{Noether structure.}
From the homogeneity of the Euclidean arena~$\mathcal{M}^4$~(P1) and
the absence of explicit $X^A$-dependence in the action~(P3), Noether's
theorem~\cite{Noether1918} gives a conserved energy-momentum tensor:
\begin{equation}
  T^{AB}_{(\Phi)} = \partial^A\Phi\,\partial^B\Phi
  - \delta^{AB}\mathcal{L}_\Phi,
  \qquad \partial_A T^{AB}_{(\Phi)} = 0.
\end{equation}
$O(4)$ rotational symmetry~(P2) additionally produces angular Noether
currents $J^{C,AB} = X^A T^{CB} - X^B T^{CA}$.
Conserved structures do not appear as separate postulates: they follow
necessarily from the symmetry-compatible extremal principle.
The full derivation with explicit formulae for both sets of currents,
including the orientation-field sector, is given in
Appendix~\ref{app:noether_full}.


\paragraph{Lorentzian interpretation of the Noether structure
in the ordered branch.}
In homogeneous ordered backgrounds, the unbroken translation and
rotation symmetries imply the corresponding conserved Noether currents.
Within the emergent Lorentzian EFT, these are interpreted as the
energy--momentum currents and the three spatial angular-momentum
currents.
Whether the associated global charges are well defined depends, as
usual, on the background and boundary conditions.

This is a Lorentzian reinterpretation of the Euclidean Noether data,
not yet a full derivation of the exact Poincar\'e group as a symmetry
of the completed low-energy theory~(Level~A conservation laws, with
Level~B Lorentzian interpretation).

\paragraph{Anisotropy of the background Noether tensor.}
For the ordered background
$\langle\partial_A\Phi\rangle = u_0\,\delta_{Aw}$,
with background potential contribution
$V_{\rm bg}\equiv V[\Phi_{\rm bg}]$,
the canonical Noether tensor is anisotropic:
\begin{align}
  T_{\rm bg}^{ww} &= \frac{u_0^2}{2} - V_{\rm bg},
  \label{eq:Tww}\\
  T_{\rm bg}^{ij} &= -\delta^{ij}\!\left(
    \frac{u_0^2}{2} + V_{\rm bg}\right),
  \qquad i,j = 1,2,3,
  \label{eq:Tij}\\
  T_{\rm bg}^{wi} &= 0.
  \label{eq:Twi}
\end{align}
Hence, for each fixed spatial direction~$i$ (no sum),
\begin{equation}
  \boxed{T_{\rm bg}^{ww} - T_{\rm bg}^{ii} = u_0^2.}
  \label{eq:T_anisotropy}
\end{equation}
This anisotropy is a direct consequence of the gradient
condensate~(P4) and is \emph{independent of the background potential
contribution $V_{\rm bg}$}.
The ordered condensate therefore distinguishes the preferred
$w$-direction from the transverse directions already at the level
of the Noether tensor.
After Lorentzian continuation this anisotropy is reinterpreted as the
difference between temporal and spatial components in the effective
energy--momentum description~(Level~A computation; Lorentzian
interpretation Level~B).

This is consistent with the arrow-of-time analysis of
Section~\ref{sec:arrow_of_time}: the same ordered background that
distinguishes the preferred branch direction also distinguishes the
temporal and spatial components of the effective stress tensor.

\paragraph{Background stress anisotropy and effective vacuum
interpretation.}
Equations~\eqref{eq:Tww}--\eqref{eq:Tij} show that the ordered
background carries an anisotropic stress pattern.
If one adopts the Lorentzian branch interpretation in which
$T_{\rm bg}^{ww}$ is read as an energy-density component and
$T_{\rm bg}^{ii}$ (no sum) as minus the corresponding pressure,
then one is naturally led to the effective identifications
\begin{equation}
  \rho_{\rm bg}^{\rm eff} = \frac{u_0^2}{2} - V_{\rm bg},
  \qquad
  p_{\rm bg}^{\rm eff} = -\!\left(\frac{u_0^2}{2}
  + V_{\rm bg}\right).
  \label{eq:rho_p_bg}
\end{equation}
Formally this gives
\begin{equation}
  w_{\rm bg}^{\rm eff}
  =
  \frac{p_{\rm bg}^{\rm eff}}{\rho_{\rm bg}^{\rm eff}}
  =
  -\,\frac{u_0^2/2 + V_{\rm bg}}{u_0^2/2 - V_{\rm bg}}.
  \label{eq:w_bg}
\end{equation}
The algebraic formula is immediate once the Lorentzian interpretation
is adopted, but its physical vacuum-energy meaning depends on the
background subtraction and EFT matching used in the macroscopic
gravitational sector.
For this reason, the expression above should be read as a structurally
motivated effective relation rather than as a standalone prediction
for the observed dark-energy equation of state.

\paragraph{Bridge to the gravitational source tensor.}
The Noether tensor of the microscopic $\Phi$-medium provides the
natural starting point for the effective source tensor used in the
macroscopic gravitational description.
However, the explicit bridge from the Euclidean microscopic tensor to
the Lorentzian source tensor entering the generalised Einstein
equations requires coarse graining, background subtraction, and EFT
matching.
Accordingly, the bridge is structurally well motivated, but its
explicit realisation is a Level~B statement rather than a strict
Level~A derivation~(developed in Section~\ref{sec:mp_noether}).

\paragraph{Ward identities and the quantum programme.}
The Noether conservation laws suggest that Ward-type identities should
exist in the coherent quantum sector as well.
In a completed quantum reconstruction of ECT, such identities may help
constrain the effective action and its symmetry structure.
At present, however, neither the explicit form of these identities nor
their relation to effective general covariance has been derived.
This remains part of the open quantum programme.

\paragraph{Constrained structure of possible Lorentz violation.}
The ordered-branch construction suggests that Lorentz-violating
effects, if present, should exhibit correlated rather than arbitrary
structure: they are expected to reflect the condensate anisotropy
associated with the preferred branch direction.
In that sense ECT points toward a more constrained pattern than a
fully general SME-style parameterisation.
A precise mapping of the effective LV operator basis remains open.

\paragraph{Sector dependence of physical interpretation.}
The same variational backbone underlies multiple effective descriptions.
What depends on the later development of the theory is not the
existence of Noether structure, but the physical interpretation of the
resulting currents and charges in particular sectors.
In some sectors the corresponding observables already admit a clear
physical reading; in others --- especially the gauge and particle
sectors --- the exact mapping to late-time observed charges and
couplings remains incomplete.

\paragraph{Inheritance by the ordered branches.}
Spontaneous symmetry breaking $O(4)\to O(3)$ reorganises the medium
into reduced sectors but does not destroy the variational backbone.
Both the geometric and the coherent-topological branch inherit
variational logic from the underlying medium; their effective equations
of motion are reductions of~\eqref{eq:variational}, not independent
postulates:
\begin{equation}
  \delta S_E[\Phi]=0
  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad
  \delta S_{\rm eff}=0
  \label{eq:delta_Seff_zero}
\end{equation}
after branch reduction, coarse-graining, or EFT matching.
This is Level~A/B: the reduction scheme is structural, but the explicit
form of $S_{\rm eff}$ for each branch must still be determined by EFT
matching~(Appendix~\ref{app:variational_reduction}).

\paragraph{Relation to the arrow of time.}
The variational principle does not conflict with the emergence of
a thermodynamic arrow of time~(Section~\ref{sec:arrow_of_time}).
At the fine-grained level the dynamics remains extremal.
Irreversibility arises only at the coarse-grained level, where the full
microscopic branch information is no longer tracked.
ECT therefore does not obtain entropy growth by breaking extremality;
rather, it obtains effective irreversibility by combining extremal
fine-grained dynamics with multiplicity~(S6), inhomogeneous
ordering~(S8), and coarse graining of branch evolution~(Level~B).

\paragraph{Relation to \texorpdfstring{$S_0$}{S0} and the classical/quantum divide.}
The variational backbone also links naturally to the pre-quantum action
scale $S_0$~(Section~\ref{sec:hbar_status}).
Extremality determines which histories are dynamically selected;
$S_0$ controls when action differences remain phase-sensitive.
When $|\Delta S|\gg S_0^{\rm EFT}$, relative phases oscillate rapidly and an
effectively classical, extremal description dominates.
When $|\Delta S|\sim S_0$, phase sensitivity cannot be neglected and
nearby histories contribute nontrivially.
ECT therefore relates classical extremality and quantum behaviour
through the same action framework, with $S_0$ governing the transition
between the two regimes~(Level~B; Section~\ref{sec:hbar_status}).

\paragraph{Coarse graining and effective extremality.}
Coarse graining does not eliminate the fundamental variational
principle; it changes the level of description.
Many distinct fine-grained extremal configurations correspond to the
same effective coarse-grained state, and this is precisely why
macroscopic irreversibility and entropy growth can coexist with
an underlying variational dynamics.
Some effective sectors of ECT can still be written in extremal form
even when their exact derivation from the bare action has not yet
been completed~(Level~B closure).

\paragraph{Defect and topological sectors.}
Because S8 and S9 allow inhomogeneous ordering and topological sectors,
defects, domain walls, and defect-adjacent configurations must also be
regarded as admissible members of the overall variational framework.
What changes is not whether they are governed by extremality, but
whether a simple smooth effective reduction is available.
Defect sectors can therefore be structurally natural in ECT without
being fully integrated into the present smooth low-energy EFT.

\paragraph{Comparison with other variational frameworks.}

\emph{Ordinary classical field theory~\cite{Landau1987Fields}:}
the action principle is usually taken as the formal starting point of
dynamics on a pre-existing spacetime.
In ECT the role of the action is deeper: it selects realised
configurations of a pre-spacetime medium.

\emph{Condensed-matter effective actions~\cite{Volovik2003}:}
in condensed matter, collective modes are described by effective actions
inherited from a more microscopic substrate.
ECT follows the same structural logic, but with the medium more
fundamental than spacetime itself.

\emph{Stationary phase and path-integral logic~\cite{FeynmanHibbs1965}:}
the relation between extremality and $S_0$ mirrors stationary-phase
dominance in the path integral.
ECT reproduces this logic structurally: extremality governs the
backbone of realised histories, while $S_0$ controls when neighbouring
histories remain phase-relevant.

\paragraph{Status summary.}
\begin{center}
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.2}
\begin{tabular}{p{6.3cm}p{2.0cm}p{5.0cm}}
\hline
Statement & Status & Comment \\
\hline
Physical configurations selected variationally
  & A & DP \\
Noether $T^{AB}$: existence and conservation
  & A & P1 + P3 + DP \\
Noether $J^{C,AB}$: existence and conservation
  & A & P1 + P2 + DP \\
Symmetry-compatible action implies Noether structure
  & A & Standard variational logic \\
Lorentzian interpretation of translation currents
  as energy--momentum
  & B & Ordered-branch Lorentzian EFT interpretation \\
Background anisotropy
  $T_{\rm bg}^{ww}-T_{\rm bg}^{ii}=u_0^2$
  & A & Direct computation;
    independent of $V_{\rm bg}$ \\
Effective vacuum equation-of-state interpretation
  & B & Requires Lorentzian reading + background
    subtraction/matching \\
Restriction of the variational problem preserves
  extremality
  & A & Branch-restricted variational problem \\
Explicit reduced branch functional $S_{\rm eff}$
  & B & EFT matching needed \\
Coherent branch inherits variational structure
  & B & In reduced branch EFT \\
Structural bridge to macroscopic source tensor
  & B & Coarse graining + EFT matching;
    \S\ref{sec:mp_noether} \\
Constrained LV structure from condensate anisotropy
  & B & Structural expectation; explicit basis open \\
Ward identities in the coherent quantum sector
  & C (open) & Quantum reconstruction not complete \\
Defect sectors compatible with variational framework
  & B & Not fully reduced to smooth EFT \\
Irreversibility compatible with extremality
  & B & Through coarse graining~(\S\ref{sec:arrow_of_time}) \\
Classical limit as extremal dominance ($|\Delta S|\gg S_0^{\rm EFT}$)
  & B & Linked to \S\ref{sec:hbar_status} \\
Full derivation of all effective branch functionals
  & Open & Not yet completed \\
Late-time charge reconstruction in all sectors
  & Open & Depends on gauge/quantum programme \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

\subsection{Remark on defect sectors beyond the smooth coherent sector}
\label{sec:defect_sectors}

The derived coherent-sector rule~BR1 restricts the present section to the
\emph{defect-free} smooth coherent sector: configurations with integer
winding numbers, non-vanishing amplitude~$\rho$, and smooth orientation
variable~$n_A$.
This restriction does not forbid defect configurations ontologically:
the topological completeness property~S9 ensures that all topological
sectors compatible with the vacuum manifold $O(4)/O(3)\simeq S^3$
remain admissible parts of the theory.
Configurations not belonging to the coherent sector include:
\begin{itemize}
  \item \textit{Vortex cores}: configurations with $\rho=0$ where
    phase~$\theta$ is undefined;
  \item \textit{Domain walls}: configurations with discontinuities
    or sharp changes of~$n_A$;
  \item \textit{Instantons}: non-trivial topological configurations
    in Euclidean space describing tunnelling between different
    vacuum sectors~\cite{Belavin1975}.
\end{itemize}
ECT basics already indicates the existence of this richer space
of states. The smooth coherent-sector restriction does not ``artificially eliminate''
any physics; it selects a sector and thereby \emph{predicts} the
existence of a broader structure beyond it.

\subsection{Conclusions of ECT basics}
\label{sec:basics_summary}

ECT basics establishes the following common foundation for both branches:
\begin{enumerate}
  \item Connected four-dimensional Euclidean arena with initially
    equivalent directions and points~(P1; ontology layer S1--S3);
  \item Local $O(4)$-invariant scalar microdynamics and the minimal
    variational realisation of the $\Phi$-medium~(P2--P3, DP);
  \item Variational selection of physically realised $\Phi$-configurations
    and the origin of emergent extremal dynamics~(DP,
    Section~\ref{sec:noether});
  \item Gradient-ordered vacuum branch with spontaneous
    $O(4)\to O(3)$ breaking~(P4);
  \item Admissibility of non-trivial and non-uniform ordered
    configurations~(P5);
  \item Smooth coherent branch rule~BR1 as a derived restriction within
    the broader topologically complete configuration space~(S9);
  \item Effective Lorentzian kinetic tensor of the broken phase;
  \item Clear sector separation between the heavy amplitude mode~$\sigma$,
    the constrained soft orientation sector (one longitudinal scalar~$\chi$
    at Level~B; Section~\ref{sec:goldstone_integrability_main}),
    and the macroscopic $\phi$-sector~(Section~\ref{sec:sector_map});
  \item Effective propagation speed~$c_*$ in the minimal broken-phase EFT;
  \item Four-level parameter hierarchy: bare, vacuum, EFT, coherent;
  \item Gravitational stiffness scale~$M_G$ matched to $G_N$;
  \item Pre-quantum action scale~$S_0$;
  \item \textbf{A layered origin of the arrow of time:}
    the ordered branch selects a preferred geometric time direction
    (Level~A), while the low-entropy interpretation of the initial
    ordered state and the structural naturalness of entropy growth
    follow at Level~B from multiplicity~S6, inhomogeneous
    ordering~S8, and coarse-grained branch evolution
    (Section~\ref{sec:arrow_of_time});
  \item \textbf{A pre-quantum action scale $S_0$:}
    the coherent-topological branch naturally generates a distinguished
    action scale through phase single-valuedness and minimal winding~(BR1),
    providing the strongest current bridge between ECT and the quantum
    sector, and separating the classical from the phase-sensitive regime
    (Section~\ref{sec:hbar_status});
  \item \textbf{A variational backbone for realised physics:}
    ECT distinguishes ontological admissibility from physical
    realisation, and selects realised configurations through a
    symmetry-compatible extremal principle; the ordered branches
    inherit this structure in reduced form, and it remains compatible
    with both the arrow of time and the classical/quantum divide
    (Section~\ref{sec:noether}).
\end{enumerate}

What is \emph{not yet completed} in ECT basics:
\begin{itemize}
  \item Full macroscopic gravitational and cosmological dynamics;
  \item Full quantum kinematics and interpretation;
  \item Identification $S_0=\hbar$ at the level of a strict derivation
    (present status: strongly motivated at Level~B via phase periodicity
    and winding sectors; full derivation deferred to
    Appendix~\ref{app:action_scale_S0} and Section~\ref{sec:quantum});
  \item Exact inverse-Higgs analysis and the final counting of
    independent propagating soft modes;
  \item Explicit matching of the orientation-sector stiffness
    coefficient $Z_n$ to the bare condensate parameters of P3;
  \item Pseudo-Goldstone mass-generation mechanism for the soft
    orientation sector~(OP-GM);
  \item Relation of the broken-phase orientation sector to any
    electroweak localisation mechanism~(OP-EW);
  \item Three-scale hierarchy: mechanism generating $v_2$ from $u_0$
    (OP-EW); first-principles derivation of $g^\dagger$ (OP-galactic);
    see Section~\ref{sec:three_scales} and Appendix~\ref{app:second_transition};
  \item Full derivation of all effective extremal principles in the
    emergent Lorentzian and coherent sectors from the microscopic
    Euclidean functional;
  \item Nonlinear closure of each branch;
  \item \textbf{Microscopic irreversibility and the full second law:}
    ECT provides a structural Level~B account of entropy growth
    and a geometric time orientation at Level~A, but a complete
    first-principles derivation of the microscopic decoherence
    kernel, H-theorem, and irreversible thermodynamics remains
    open~(OP1, OP5, OP24; Section~\ref{sec:arrow_of_time});
  \item \textbf{Full quantum reconstruction from $S_0$:}
    ECT motivates $S_0^{\rm EFT}$ as the precursor of $\hbar$ and suggests
    the phase factor $e^{iS/S_0^{\rm EFT}}$, but the full derivation of the
    quantum measure, Born rule, operator algebra, and Hilbert-space
    structure remains open~(Section~\ref{sec:hbar_status});
  \item \textbf{Full reduction of all effective extremal sectors:}
    while the variational backbone is fundamental, a complete
    first-principles derivation of every effective branch functional
    and every late-time observable Noether charge remains open
    (Section~\ref{sec:noether}).
\end{itemize}

\begin{table}[H]
\centering\small
\caption{Structural branching of ECT after the present ECT-basics stage.}
\label{tab:ect_branching}
\begin{tabular}{p{3cm}p{5cm}p{4.5cm}}
\hline\hline
\textbf{Branch} & \textbf{Primary variables} & \textbf{Developed in} \\
\hline
Geometric\\(Macroscopic Physics)
  & $g^{\rm eff}_{AB}$, $n_A$, $M_G$, and long-wavelength
    background variations of the ordered branch
  & Gravity, cosmology, galactic phenomenology, observational tests \\
Coherent-topological\\(Quantum Sector)
  & $\Phi_{\rm eff}$, $\theta$, $S_0$, coherent winding sectors,
    and coherent correlators
  & Action scale quantisation, wave dynamics, quantum kinematics, decoherence, probability \\
\hline\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\paragraph{Roadmap.}
The two major parts of the theory follow naturally.
The present basics stage is sufficient to define both branches
structurally, although several quantitative problems of the broken
phase remain explicitly open.
\textit{Macroscopic Physics} (Section~\ref{sec:macroscopic} onwards):
effective gravity, vacuum long-wavelength response, cosmology,
galactic phenomenology, and observational tests.
\textit{Quantum Sector} (Section~\ref{sec:quantum} onwards):
action scale quantisation, wave dynamics of coherent excitations,
canonical quantum structure, decoherence, and the status of
probabilistic interpretation.


% ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
\subsection{Foundational consequences and branch-selection rules}
\label{sec:foundational_consequences}

For later use it is convenient to collect the main consequences of the
foundational construction developed in Part~I.

\begin{enumerate}
  \item \textbf{Ordered-branch rule.}
  The physically realised vacuum belongs to an ordered branch with
  non-zero gradient condensate,
  $\langle \partial_A\Phi\rangle \neq 0$,
  which spontaneously breaks $O(4)\to O(3)$.

  \item \textbf{Amplitude--orientation decomposition.}
  On the ordered branch the condensate gradient admits the decomposition
  \[
  \partial_A\Phi = u\,n_A,
  \qquad
  u\ge 0,
  \qquad
  n_A n_A = 1.
  \]
  Because $Q_A=\partial_A\Phi$ is an exact one-form, $u$ and $n_A$
  are not independent: they satisfy the integrability condition
  $\partial_A(u n_B)-\partial_B(u n_A)=0$, which reduces the
  orientation sector to one longitudinal soft scalar at linear order
  (Level~B; Appendix~\ref{app:goldstone_integrability}).

  \item \textbf{Uniqueness of the broken-phase kinetic tensor.}
  Local symmetric rank-2 tensors compatible with the residual $O(3)$
  symmetry take the form
  \[
  K^{AB}=\beta\,\delta^{AB}-\alpha\,n^A n^B.
  \]
  Here $n_A$ is the derived ordered-branch orientation variable,
  not an independent microscopic field (Section~\ref{sec:sector_map}).

  \item \textbf{Signature classification rule.}
  Depending on $(\alpha,\beta)$, the broken-phase kinetic operator has
  Euclidean, degenerate, or Lorentzian signature.

  \item \textbf{Macroscopic viability rule.}
  Only the hyperbolic Lorentzian branch ($\alpha>\beta$) is admissible
  as the macroscopic causal branch of the observed universe; this is
  selected by physical viability, not imposed as an extra postulate.

  \item \textbf{Smooth coherent-sector rule (BR1).}
  In the defect-free coherent infrared branch with single-valued
  collective phase, the winding number is necessarily integer:
  \[
  \oint_{\mathcal{C}}\partial_A\theta\,dX^A = 2\pi n,
  \qquad n\in\mathbb{Z}.
  \]

  \item \textbf{Two-branch rule.}
  The ordered condensate framework supports two effective developments:
  the geometric macroscopic branch and the coherent-topological branch.
  They arise from the same underlying medium but retain different
  classes of observables under coarse-graining.
\end{enumerate}

These statements will be used repeatedly in the subsequent development
of the gravitational, cosmological, astrophysical, and quantum sectors.

\section{From legacy quantum language to the coherent-topological branch}
\label{sec:quantum_old}

This section explains how the older quantum-mechanical language of ECT
is reorganised inside the present coherent-topological branch.
It is not a second independent quantum theory inside the article.
Its purpose is transitional and interpretive:
to preserve continuity with earlier drafts while making clear how the
new Level~A / Level~B / Open discipline changes the meaning of the
claims.

\subsection*{Why the old standalone quantum section is no longer kept}

In earlier drafts, several quantum-mechanical statements were grouped
together in one large section:
loop quantisation, Schr\"odinger-type dynamics, canonical commutators,
Born-type probabilities, decoherence, vacuum effects, and black-hole
information logic.
That organisation is no longer optimal.
The present article distributes these topics according to the actual
internal architecture of the coherent branch:
phase/winding structure, pre-quantum action scale, wave reduction,
canonical structure, uncertainty, statistics, decoherence,
vacuum-response effects, defect sectors, and strong-field horizon logic
are now developed in distinct parts of the Quantum Sector itself.

This is not merely editorial reordering.
It reflects the fact that not all such statements have the same status:
some are structural, some are effective, and some remain open.
The older unified presentation compressed these differences too strongly.

\subsection*{The new quantum logic in one chain}

The coherent-topological branch is organised by the following sequence:
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)]
  \item a phase-sensitive condensate sector exists once the ordered
  branch is selected;
  \item single-valuedness of the order parameter gives winding sectors
  and a minimal loop action;
  \item this defines the structural action scale $S_0$ of the coherent
  branch;
  \item reduced wave dynamics takes a Schr\"odinger-type form with
  $S_0$ in the role of the action parameter;
  \item canonical structure, uncertainty relations, and probability
  assignments are then addressed at the level appropriate to their
  status;
  \item subsystem--environment splitting yields decoherence and
  effective reduced-state irreversibility without fundamental loss of
  global coherence;
  \item vacuum-response phenomena and black-hole thermality are
  interpreted as organised probes of the same coherent vacuum structure.
\end{enumerate}

\subsection*{What has been strengthened relative to the old presentation}

The new Quantum Sector strengthens the framework in several ways.

First, the existence of the coherent action scale $S_0$, the winding
structure, and the subsystem--environment logic are now stated
structurally and separated from later physical identifications.

Second, the article no longer presents the Born rule, the full Pauli
principle, or the final identification $S_0=\hbar$ as unconditional
bare-postulate theorems.

Third, the horizon-thermality discussion is now integrated with the
general reduced-state logic rather than treated as an isolated
exception.

Fourth, the coherent-topological branch is now visibly parallel to the
macroscopic branch: both are branches of one ordered condensate, rather
than independent theories awkwardly glued together.

\subsection*{What becomes weaker, and why that is acceptable}

The price of the new discipline is that some statements once presented
as final derivations are now explicitly conditional.
This is intentional.
The current article does not claim that the old quantum section was
simply wrong; rather, it claims that its status assignments were too
strong.
The new formulation is therefore more modest but also more reliable.

In particular:
\begin{itemize}
  \item $S_0$ exists structurally, but $S_0=\hbar$ is not yet a
  completed Level~A theorem;
  \item Schr\"odinger-type reduction is structurally strong, but the
  completed observational quantum equation is still conditional on
  further identifications;
  \item the Born rule is stronger than a mere guess, but still not an
  unconditional bare-postulate theorem;
  \item black-hole information loss is ruled out as a fundamental
  non-unitary resolution, but the constructive strong-field mechanism is
  not yet complete.
\end{itemize}

\subsection*{Map of old topics to the present Quantum Sector}

For continuity with earlier drafts, the correspondence is:
\begin{itemize}
  \item loop/winding quantisation
    $\rightarrow$ Sections~\ref{sec:qs_phase_dynamics}
    and~\ref{sec:qs_hbar};
  \item Schr\"odinger-type reduction
    $\rightarrow$ Section~\ref{sec:qs_wave};
  \item canonical structure and commutators
    $\rightarrow$ Section~\ref{sec:qs_ccr};
  \item uncertainty and extremal action
    $\rightarrow$ Sections~\ref{sec:qs_uncertainty}
    and~\ref{sec:qs_extremal_action};
  \item conservation and statistics
    $\rightarrow$ Sections~\ref{sec:qs_conservation},
    \ref{sec:qs_statistics}, and~\ref{sec:qs_dirac};
  \item decoherence, influence functionals, and effective entropy
    $\rightarrow$ Section~\ref{sec:qs_decoherence};
  \item Born-type probability assignment
    $\rightarrow$ Section~\ref{sec:qs_born};
  \item vacuum response (Casimir, particle production, Unruh, Hawking)
    $\rightarrow$ Sections~\ref{sec:qs_vacuum_intro}--
    \ref{sec:qs_vacuum_summary};
  \item defect sectors and dark-sector bridge
    $\rightarrow$ Section~\ref{sec:qs_defects};
  \item black holes and information-loss logic
    $\rightarrow$ Section~\ref{sec:qs_black_holes}.
\end{itemize}

\subsection*{Compatibility labels}

To preserve backward compatibility with earlier internal references,
the labels
\label{sec:loops}
\label{sec:CCR}
\label{par:real_vs_complex}
are retained here as compatibility anchors only.
They should not be interpreted as signalling surviving independent
legacy derivations.
Their physical content is now developed in the coherent-topological
Quantum Sector proper.

\subsection*{Interpretive conclusion}

This section should therefore be read as a bridge, not as a second,
parallel quantum theory inside the paper.
The old quantum language has not been discarded; it has been absorbed,
reclassified, and redistributed into a more precise coherent-branch
architecture.
That is why the article becomes shorter here without becoming
physically poorer: the real development now lives in the new QS block,
and this section explains how to read that transition correctly.

\paragraph{Practical reading instruction.}
A reader familiar with older ECT drafts should therefore use this
section as a translation layer.
The real quantum development of the present article is no longer here,
but in the coherent-topological Quantum Sector itself.
This section exists to prevent discontinuity of interpretation, not to
recreate the older structure in parallel.


%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
\section{Consistency relations, matching conventions, and legacy notation}
\label{sec:consistency_relations}

This section records the internal consistency layer of the present
formulation.
Its purpose is not to provide a closed first-principles derivation of
all fundamental constants from the bare condensate action.
Rather, it clarifies how different scales and matching relations enter
the theory, how older shorthand notation should now be interpreted, and
which relations are structural, which are effective, and which remain
open.

\subsection*{Why this section is still needed}

Earlier ECT drafts used one shared notation layer for several
conceptually distinct tasks:
the propagation speed of the ordered branch,
the observed Newton coupling,
the pre-quantum action scale,
and various cosmological and galactic benchmark estimates were often
written in one continuous symbolic chain.
The present article no longer treats that chain as a single derivation.
But a consistency section is still useful, because the reader needs a
compact place where the logic of matching, separation of scales, and
legacy notation is stated explicitly.

\subsection*{Why separation of scales is not a cosmetic change}

The separation between $u_0$, $M_G$, $S_0$, and $g_\dagger$ is not a
mere notational refinement.
It resolves a real ambiguity of earlier ECT drafts, in which one common
symbolic layer was used simultaneously for background ordering,
gravitational response, coherent quantum action, and galactic weak-field
phenomenology.
In the present formulation these roles are no longer merged.

This matters for three reasons.
First, it prevents overclaim: a structural action scale in the coherent
branch is not automatically the observed Planck constant, and a
macroscopic gravitational stiffness is not automatically a bare
microscopic condensate amplitude.
Second, it localises open problems precisely:
the $(u_0,\alpha,\beta)\to M_G$ route and the $S_0\to\hbar$
identification are now clearly separated rather than conflated.
Third, it allows different sectors of the theory to be tested
independently:
the macroscopic tensor branch by gravitational matching and
astrophysical phenomenology, and the coherent branch by its quantum,
decoherence, and vacuum-response structure.

\subsection*{The three principal constants that must not be conflated}
\textit{Note: these are the three physical constants $c_*$, $G_N$, $\hbar$ derived from the
condensate. They are distinct from the three condensate amplitude scales
$u_0, v_2, v_{\rm gal}$ discussed in Section~\ref{sec:three_scales}.}

The present formulation separates three physically different scales.

\paragraph{(i) Ordered-branch propagation scale.}
The broken-phase tensor/kinetic structure fixes the effective
propagation velocity
\begin{equation}
  c_*^2 = \frac{\beta}{\alpha-\beta}.
\end{equation}
With the observational choice $c_*=c$, this sets the canonical
normalisation of the ordered branch.
This is the most direct and structurally clean constant relation in the
framework.

\paragraph{(ii) Macroscopic gravitational stiffness scale.}
Observed weak-field gravity is organised by the tensor EFT stiffness
parameter $M_G$, which enters through
\begin{equation}
  G_N = \frac{c_*^4}{8\pi M_G^2}.
\end{equation}
This is the correct gravitational relation in the present article.
It is a geometric EFT matching statement, not a microscopic theorem from
one condensate amplitude.
The physical content is that the observed weakness of gravity reflects
the extreme stiffness of the ordered tensor branch.

\paragraph{(iii) Coherent action scale.}
The coherent-topological branch possesses a structural action scale
$S_0$, fixed through the elementary-loop action
\begin{equation}
  S_{\rm loop,min}=2\pi S_0.
\end{equation}
Here $S_{\rm loop,min}$ is the elementary-loop action at the
characteristic core scale~(Section~\ref{sec:hbar_status}),
not a global variational minimum.
The final physical identification $S_0=\hbar$ remains a Level~B/open
step, not a completed Level~A theorem.

\subsection*{Old notation versus present reading}

\begin{center}
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.25}
\begin{tabular}{p{3.0cm}p{4.8cm}p{4.5cm}}
\hline\hline
Legacy symbol / phrase & Older compressed reading & Present disciplined reading \\
\hline
$v_0$ & One shared condensate scale & Legacy shorthand only; now split into sector-specific variables \\
$u_0$ & Often blurred with $v_0$ & Ordered gradient/background scale of the broken phase \\
$M_G$ & Often implicit or absent & Macroscopic tensor stiffness entering $G_N$ matching \\
$S_0$ & Not sharply separated & Structural coherent action scale from minimal loop action \\
$\hbar$ & Often identified too early & Final physical identification of $S_0$, still Level~B/open \\
$g_\dagger$ & Sometimes treated as quasi-fundamental & Galactic/environmental weak-field closure scale \\
\hline\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

\subsection*{Additional effective scales}

Besides the three principal scales above, the present article also uses
two further effective scales in specific sectors.

\paragraph{(iv) Ordered gradient scale $u_0$.}
The symbol $u_0$ denotes the ordered gradient scale of the broken phase.
It characterises the ordered background sector, but should not be
confused with either the macroscopic gravitational stiffness $M_G$ or
the coherent action scale $S_0$.

\paragraph{(v) Galactic/environmental weak-field scale $g_\dagger$.}
In the present critical $\phi$-branch closure, galactic phenomenology is
organised by the acceleration scale $g_\dagger$ together with the
screening/environment scales discussed in the Macroscopic Physics part.
This is a sector-specific weak-field scale, not a universal microscopic
constant.

\subsection*{Status discipline for the constants}

\paragraph{Speed of light.}
The role of $c$ is structurally stronger than that of $G_N$ or $\hbar$:
the ordered branch itself fixes a propagation speed $c_*$, and the
observed light speed corresponds to the empirical choice $c_*=c$.

\paragraph{Newton's constant.}
$G_N$ is not currently obtained as a direct bare-postulate function of
one microscopic condensate amplitude.
Its status in the present article is geometric EFT matching through
$M_G$.

\paragraph{Planck's constant.}
The coherent branch does provide a structural action scale $S_0$.
But the final identification of this scale with the observed Planck
constant is not yet unconditional.
It remains one of the central open bridges between the structural QS
framework and completed observational quantum mechanics.

\subsection*{How legacy $v_0$ notation should now be read}

Earlier drafts often wrote a single amplitude-like symbol $v_0$ in
contexts that now belong to three different sectors:
\begin{itemize}
  \item background scalar/amplitude language,
  \item macroscopic gravitational stiffness language,
  \item coherent action-scale language.
\end{itemize}
This is no longer acceptable as the primary notation of the present
article.
Whenever legacy formulas involving $v_0$ remain in historical remarks or
technical derivation contexts, they should be interpreted only as
notation inherited from an older compression of variables.
The actual physically relevant variables are now:
$u_0$ for the ordered gradient scale,
$M_G$ for macroscopic tensor stiffness,
and $S_0$ for the coherent action scale.

\subsection*{What this section does \emph{not} claim}

This section does not claim:
\begin{itemize}
  \item that $G_N$ has already been derived microscopically from bare
  P3/P4 data;
  \item that $S_0=\hbar$ has already been proved as a Level~A theorem;
  \item that older shorthand formulas should still be read as final
  results of the theory;
  \item that cosmological or galactic benchmark relations based on
  closure parameters should be confused with structural constants of the
  bare theory.
\end{itemize}
Its role is the opposite: to prevent such misreadings.

\subsection*{Where the actual derivations now live}

The reader should therefore use this section only as a consistency map.
For the macroscopic gravitational matching logic, see
Section~\ref{sec:mp_matching} and Appendix~\ref{app:GN_derivation}.
For the status of the coherent action scale and the
$S_0\leftrightarrow\hbar$ question, see
Sections~\ref{sec:qs_hbar}, \ref{sec:qs_wave}, and~\ref{sec:qs_ccr}.
For the global classification of what is Level~A, Level~B,
and Open, see Tables~\ref{tab:ect_structure}
and~\ref{tab:ect-levels}.

\subsection*{What this buys us methodologically}

The gain of the present consistency layer is not that it magically turns
all constant relations into Level~A theorems.
Its gain is methodological clarity.
The framework now distinguishes:
\begin{itemize}
  \item structural existence statements;
  \item effective matching relations;
  \item sector-dependent closure parameters;
  \item genuinely open microscopic reduction problems.
\end{itemize}
This makes the theory less rhetorically ambitious but more scientifically
usable.
In particular, it becomes possible to say exactly where additional
postulates or deeper microscopic derivations would still be needed in
order to push certain Level~B statements upward.

\paragraph{Interpretive conclusion.}
The value of the present consistency section is therefore not that it
produces a one-line derivation of all constants.
Its value is that it keeps distinct what the earlier notation blurred:
propagation speed, gravitational stiffness, coherent action scale,
ordered background scale, and galactic/environmental weak-field scale.
That conceptual separation is itself one of the key improvements of the
new ECT formulation.

\paragraph{Compatibility label.}
The compatibility anchor
\label{sec:running_coupling}
is retained here because some older internal references still point to
this part of the article.
It should be read in the present consistency/matching sense, not in the
older direct-derivation sense.
\section{Gauge Symmetries and Interactions}
\label{sec:gauge}

Gauge-like sectors in ECT are described through emergent internal
symmetry structures of extended condensate representations.
In the minimal scalar ECT basis these sectors are not yet derived
microscopically; they are introduced as structural embeddings
compatible with the coherent branch.
This section shows how $U(1)$, $SU(2)$, and $SU(3)$ gauge
structures can be \emph{embedded} by promoting global symmetries
of extended condensate representations to local ones.

\textbf{Honest framing.}
The standard gauge-principle argument (promote global $\to$ local,
introduce connection, get kinetic term) is generic: it applies to any
field theory with internal symmetries and does not uniquely select the
Standard Model gauge group $U(1)\times SU(2)\times SU(3)$.  ECT
provides a natural host for gauge sectors, but the exact SM gauge group
is not derived from first principles within the current framework ---
it must be imposed as an additional input on the internal symmetry
structure of the condensate.  The results below should therefore be
read as showing \emph{compatibility} of ECT with gauge theories, not
as a derivation of the SM gauge group from the condensate alone.

\subsection{Internal symmetries of the condensate}

In the ECT framework, gauge interactions can be embedded via local
internal symmetries of the condensate field (Level~B/C; the SM gauge
group is embedded, not derived).  The appearance of gauge fields
follows from the requirement that the condensate action admits
local phase invariance once internal structure is introduced.

\paragraph{Global phase symmetry.}

As a structural low-energy representation of the coherent branch,
consider a complex condensate field

\begin{equation}
\Phi(X) = v_0(X)\,e^{i\theta(X)} .
\end{equation}

In the \emph{emergent complex ordered sector} (see
Section~\ref{par:real_vs_complex}: the minimal ECT field is real;
phase language belongs to the two-component extension), if the phase
is shifted by a constant,

\begin{equation}
\Phi_{\rm eff} \rightarrow e^{i\alpha}\Phi_{\rm eff} ,
\end{equation}

the effective action of the ordered sector remains invariant.
This is a global $U(1)$ symmetry of the emergent complex coherent-branch
representation~(not of the minimal real scalar condensate of P3).

\paragraph{Physical interpretation of $\theta$.}
The phase field $\theta(X)$ in the complexified coherent-branch
representation $\Phi_{\rm eff} = \rho\,e^{i\theta}$ is the natural
compact angular variable of the coherent branch~(BR1), not an
independently postulated field.
Its gradient $\partial_A\theta$ defines the associated phase current,
and its single-valuedness on closed loops underlies the quantisation
condition~(Section~\ref{sec:quantum}).
When the global phase symmetry is promoted to a local one, a
gauge connection can be introduced in the standard EFT manner,
and the phase variable is absorbed into the gauge-covariant
phase description.
At the present stage, this establishes a distinguished compact
phase sector with $U(1)$-type structure.
The identification of this sector with the physical electromagnetic
field remains a Level~B structural matching; the full derivation
of QED from the minimal ECT postulates is not yet completed
(Section~\ref{sec:gauge}).

\subsection{Emergence of $U(1)$ gauge symmetry}
\label{sec:u1_emergence}

\textit{Status: three-layered.
Layer~1 (strict within the $U(1)$-extended phase EFT): global $U(1)$
symmetry, Noether current, and its conservation are Level~A consequences
of the phase action.
Layer~2 (Level~B structural): promotion to local $U(1)$ is motivated
by the physical requirement that only phase gradients, not phases
themselves, be locally observable; the resulting gauge structure follows
standard EFT logic.
Layer~3 (Level~B matching): identification of the emergent massless
gauge boson with the physical photon is a structural matching statement.
Full derivation of QED from minimal ECT postulates is not yet completed.}

\paragraph{Connection to the coherent branch and to the action-scale
programme.}
The phase field $\theta(X)$ appearing in the present section is the
same compact angular variable introduced in the $U(1)$-extended
description of the coherent-topological branch~(BR1), whose winding
structure gives rise to the distinguished EFT action scale
$S_0^{\rm EFT}$~(Section~\ref{sec:hbar_status}).
It is also the variable whose effective dynamics is developed in the
Quantum Sector~(Section~\ref{sec:qs_phase_dynamics}).
The gauge structure discussed below is therefore not an unrelated
addition, but a natural development of the same phase sector already
used in the action-scale and quantum-sector discussion.

% ============ LAYER 1 ============

\paragraph{Layer~1: global $U(1)$ symmetry and the Noether current
(Level~A within the $U(1)$-extended phase EFT).}

\emph{Global symmetry.}
In the emergent complex representation of the coherent branch
(Section~\ref{par:real_vs_complex}),
$\Phi_{\rm eff}=\rho\,e^{i\theta}$,
the effective action is invariant under constant phase shifts:
\begin{equation}
  \Phi_{\rm eff}(X) \;\to\; e^{i\alpha}\,\Phi_{\rm eff}(X),
  \qquad \alpha = \text{const}.
  \label{eq:global_U1}
\end{equation}
This global $U(1)$ symmetry is a property of the $U(1)$-extended
coherent-sector EFT, not of the minimal real scalar field of P3.

\emph{Noether current.}
By Noether's theorem applied to~\eqref{eq:global_U1}, the conserved
current is
\begin{equation}
  j^A \;\equiv\;
  \frac{-i}{2}\!\left(
    \Phi_{\rm eff}^*\,\partial^A\Phi_{\rm eff}
    - \Phi_{\rm eff}\,\partial^A\Phi_{\rm eff}^*
  \right)
  = \rho^2\,\partial^A\theta,
  \qquad
  \partial_A j^A = 0.
  \label{eq:U1_noether}
\end{equation}
This is a Level~A result within the $U(1)$-extended phase EFT:
it follows from the phase action and Noether's theorem without
additional assumptions.

\emph{Physical interpretation.}
The current~\eqref{eq:U1_noether} has a distinctive condensate
structure: it is the phase gradient $\partial^A\theta$ weighted by
the condensate density $\rho^2$.
In the ECT interpretation, this suggests that charged excitations
may be understood as phase-topological or phase-gradient structures
of the ordered medium rather than as completely independent primitive
objects.
This is structurally analogous to the supercurrent in a superfluid
or superconductor, where the current is likewise proportional to the
gradient of the order-parameter phase~\cite{Volovik2003}.

\paragraph{Gauge current and the homogeneous limit.}
For a homogeneous phase configuration $\partial_A\theta=0$ the current
vanishes, $j^A=0$.
Nonzero charge/current therefore appears only when the coherent branch
supports nontrivial phase structure.
In this sense the gauge current is an excitation property of the
ordered medium rather than a background property of the condensate
vacuum itself.

% ============ LAYER 2 ============

\paragraph{Layer~2: local $U(1)$ and gauge connection
(Level~B structural).}

\emph{Physical motivation for localisation.}
Local observables depend on phase gradients rather than on the
absolute value of the phase itself, although global phase holonomies
remain physically meaningful.
In an inhomogeneous condensate, the phase $\theta(X)$ can be redefined
locally by an arbitrary smooth function $\alpha(X)$ without changing
any local physical prediction---provided that the derivative is
simultaneously adjusted.
This is the standard motivation for gauge invariance in
condensed-matter and field-theoretic contexts.

In ECT this motivation is structural: once the coherent branch
possesses a compact phase, the localisation of the global symmetry
is a natural EFT step.
It is \emph{not} derived from P1--P5 as a theorem; it is a Level~B
structural extension motivated by the local redundancy of the
absolute phase.

\emph{Gauge connection.}
Promoting the phase transformation to a local one,
$\Phi_{\rm eff}\to e^{i\alpha(X)}\Phi_{\rm eff}$,
the ordinary derivative is no longer covariant:
\begin{equation}
  \partial_A\Phi_{\rm eff} \;\to\;
  e^{i\alpha(X)}\!\left(\partial_A\Phi_{\rm eff}
  + i(\partial_A\alpha)\,\Phi_{\rm eff}\right).
\end{equation}
To restore covariance one introduces a compensating connection
$A_A$ and defines the covariant derivative
\begin{equation}
  D_A = \partial_A - ieA_A,
  \label{eq:cov_deriv}
\end{equation}
with the gauge transformation
\begin{equation}
  A_A \;\to\; A_A + \frac{1}{e}\,\partial_A\alpha,
  \label{eq:gauge_transf_A}
\end{equation}
so that $D_A\Phi_{\rm eff}\to e^{i\alpha(X)}D_A\Phi_{\rm eff}$.
The constant $e$ is the gauge coupling.
In the present formulation it is a \emph{free EFT parameter},
not derived from the condensate data.

\emph{Kinetic term and its uniqueness.}
The gauge-invariant field strength is
\begin{equation}
  F_{AB} = \partial_A A_B - \partial_B A_A.
  \label{eq:F_AB}
\end{equation}
This is a standard EFT uniqueness statement at leading derivative
order: among parity-even gauge-invariant terms with at most two
derivatives, the canonical choice is
\begin{equation}
  S_{\rm gauge} = -\frac{1}{4}\int d^4X\;F_{AB}\,F^{AB}.
  \label{eq:Maxwell_action}
\end{equation}
The overall normalisation of $S_{\rm gauge}$ (i.e.\ the value of~$e$)
is not fixed by this uniqueness argument; it remains a free matching
parameter~(Level~B).

% ============ LAYER 3 ============

\paragraph{Layer~3: identification with the photon
(Level~B matching).}

In this framework the observed masslessness of the photon is naturally
consistent with the fact that the coherent-branch $U(1)$ remains
unbroken.
If that $U(1)$ were instead Higgsed, a mass term would be expected
through the Anderson--Higgs mechanism.

The identification of this massless $U(1)$ boson with the physical
photon is a Level~B structural matching: the coherent phase mode is
the most natural condensate candidate for an emergent electromagnetic
gauge degree of freedom.
The full derivation of QED---including the precise form of the
matter--gauge coupling, the running of $e$, and the anomalous magnetic
moment---is not yet completed from P1--P5 alone.

\paragraph{Holonomy and phase compactness.}
For $\Phi_{\rm eff}=\rho\,e^{i\theta}$ the gauge transformation
$\Phi_{\rm eff}\to e^{i\alpha(X)}\Phi_{\rm eff}$ acts on the phase as
$\theta\to\theta+\alpha(X)$.
Accordingly, the gauge-invariant phase gradient is
\begin{equation}
  \mathcal{D}_A\theta \;\equiv\; \partial_A\theta - eA_A.
  \label{eq:gauge_inv_phase_gradient}
\end{equation}
The compact periodicity $\theta\sim\theta+2\pi$ then implies that
the gauge-covariant phase holonomy around a closed loop must be
quantised:
\begin{equation}
  \oint_\gamma \mathcal{D}_A\theta\,dX^A = 2\pi n,
  \qquad n\in\mathbb{Z}.
  \label{eq:holonomy}
\end{equation}
Using~\eqref{eq:gauge_inv_phase_gradient}, this yields the standard
dimensionless holonomy condition
\begin{equation}
  e\oint_\gamma A_A\,dX^A = 2\pi n
  \label{eq:flux_quantisation}
\end{equation}
up to the choice of sign convention.
This is the usual compact-phase quantisation condition.
Its possible relation to the action scale $S_0^{\rm EFT}$ requires
an additional normalisation assumption and is not derived here.

\paragraph{Superfluid analogy.}
The $U(1)$ gauge structure of ECT is closest in spirit to the gauge
emergence in superfluid and superconductor
physics~\cite{Volovik2003}.
In both cases:
\begin{itemize}
  \item a compact phase variable arises from the ordered medium;
  \item single-valuedness of the phase forces quantised circulation
    or flux;
  \item the gauge connection enters as the natural compensating field
    for local phase redundancy;
  \item the masslessness or massiveness of the gauge boson depends on
    whether the $U(1)$ phase symmetry is preserved or broken.
\end{itemize}
In a superfluid, the $U(1)$ is global and there is no dynamical photon.
In a superconductor, the $U(1)$ is gauged and the photon acquires a
mass (Meissner effect).
In ECT, the coherent-branch $U(1)$ is taken to be gauged and unbroken,
which is structurally consistent with a massless photon.
The analogy is structural, not exact: the ECT condensate is not a
material medium but the pre-spacetime ordered background.

\paragraph{What is needed for Level~A.}
To promote the $U(1)$ gauge structure from Level~B embedding to Level~A
derivation, the following programme would need to be completed:
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)]
  \item derive the \emph{necessity} of local gauge invariance from the
    condensate dynamics, not merely its compatibility;
  \item show that $F_{AB}F^{AB}$ is the unique admissible kinetic term
    within the condensate EFT (currently a standard EFT statement,
    not derived from condensate structure);
  \item derive the coupling constant $e$ from condensate parameters;
  \item demonstrate that charged matter couples correctly to $A_A$
    with the observed electromagnetic vertex structure.
\end{enumerate}
None of these steps has been completed.
The $U(1)$ sector is the \emph{strongest gauge-embedding result}
currently available in ECT, but it remains an emergent phase--gauge
correspondence rather than a fully completed derivation of QED.

\paragraph{Status summary.}

\begin{center}
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.2}
\begin{tabular}{p{6.0cm}p{2.0cm}p{5.0cm}}
\hline
Statement & Status & Comment \\
\hline
\multicolumn{3}{l}{\textit{Layer~1: global $U(1)$ in the phase EFT}} \\
Global $U(1)$ symmetry of the coherent-branch
  phase action
  & B & $U(1)$-extension of real $\Phi$ \\
Noether current $j^A=\rho^2\partial^A\theta$ and its
  conservation
  & A & Within $U(1)$-extended phase EFT \\
Gauge current vanishes in homogeneous limit
  & A & $\partial_A\theta=0 \Rightarrow j^A=0$ \\
\hline

  rather than absolute phase
  & B & Kinematic interpretation within the
    local phase EFT \\
Promotion to local $U(1)$: gauge connection $A_A$
  & B & Structural EFT step, not derived from P1--P5 \\

  & B & Standard EFT uniqueness \\

  $U(1)$

    $S_0^{\rm EFT}$ not derived \\

\subsection{Non-Abelian gauge structure: structural embedding}
\label{sec:nonabelian_embedding}

\textit{Status: structural embedding, Level~B/C.
The non-Abelian gauge sector is not derived from the minimal scalar
ECT postulates P1--P5; it is shown to be structurally admissible
if the condensate possesses internal multiplet structure.
The Standard Model gauge group is embedded, not derived.}

\paragraph{General non-Abelian framework.}

If the condensate possesses internal degrees of freedom forming a
multiplet
\begin{equation}
  \Phi = (\Phi_1,\Phi_2,\dots,\Phi_N)^T,
\end{equation}
and the action is invariant under global $SU(N)$ rotations
$\Phi\to U\Phi$, then promoting these to local transformations
$U(X)$ requires the introduction of gauge connections.
The covariant derivative is
\begin{equation}
  D_A = \partial_A - ig\,A_A^a T^a,
  \label{eq:nonabelian_cov_deriv}
\end{equation}
where $T^a$ are the generators and $A_A^a$ are the associated
gauge fields.
The gauge-invariant field strength tensor is
\begin{equation}
  F_{AB}^a = \partial_A A_B^a - \partial_B A_A^a
  + g\,f^{abc}\,A_A^b A_B^c,
  \label{eq:nonabelian_F}
\end{equation}
where $f^{abc}$ are the structure constants of the group,
The canonical parity-even gauge-field action is
\begin{equation}
  S_{\rm gauge} = -\frac{1}{4}
  \int d^4X\;F_{AB}^a\,F^{a\,AB}.
  \label{eq:YM_action}
\end{equation}
This shows structural compatibility of ECT with non-Abelian gauge
theories at leading derivative order; it does not derive the specific
gauge group from the condensate.

\paragraph{The algebraic bridge:
$\mathfrak{so}(3)\cong\mathfrak{su}(2)$.}
After the spacetime symmetry breaking $O(4)\to O(3)$, the residual
unbroken rotation algebra of the ordered branch is
\begin{equation}
  \mathfrak{so}(3) \;\cong\; \mathfrak{su}(2).
  \label{eq:so3_su2_iso}
\end{equation}
This is an isomorphism of Lie algebras, not an identification of
the global groups $SO(3)$ and $SU(2)$.
Among the Standard Model simple gauge factors, $SU(2)$ is the only
one whose Lie algebra has a direct algebraic analogue in the residual
spacetime-rotation algebra of the broken phase.
This is an algebraic bridge, not yet a derivation of the electroweak
sector.
Its possible relation to chirality and the selection of $SU(2)_L$
is discussed separately in Section~\ref{sec:chirality}.

\paragraph{Accommodation of Standard Model gauge groups.}
Within this framework the gauge interactions of the Standard Model
can be \emph{accommodated} as arising from internal symmetries of
the condensate:
\begin{itemize}
  \item $U(1)$: the coherent-branch phase symmetry
    (Section~\ref{sec:u1_emergence}); structurally the strongest
    gauge-embedding result, with a distinguished Noether current
    and superfluid analogy.
  \item $SU(2)$: algebraically distinguished by the isomorphism
    $\mathfrak{so}(3)\cong\mathfrak{su}(2)$; a structural
    embedding for a weak-interaction-like sector if a second
    condensate transition exists
    (Section~\ref{sec:ew_embedding}).
  \item $SU(3)$: requires an additional colour-triplet internal
    structure with no spacetime counterpart; this remains a
    model-level extension~(Level~C).
\end{itemize}
The coupling constants $g$, $g'$, and $g_s$ are all free EFT
parameters in the present formulation; none is derived from the
condensate data.
At the present stage, neither the values nor the running of
$(g,g',g_s)$ are derived from condensate microphysics.
They remain EFT matching parameters.

\subsection{Electroweak-like embedding and its current status}
\label{sec:ew_embedding}

\textit{Status: structural embedding, Level~B/C.
The electroweak-like sector is not derived from minimal ECT; it is
shown to be structurally admissible if a second condensate transition
$O(3)\to O(2)$ is realised.
The scale $v_2$ is a matching constraint, not a prediction.
Connection to ECT basics: Section~\ref{sec:three_scales},
Appendix~\ref{app:second_transition}.}

Beyond the Abelian phase sector, one may ask whether the ordered
condensate admits an additional non-Abelian sector analogous to the
electroweak interaction.
At the present stage this must be formulated cautiously.

\paragraph{ECT-specific structural motivation.}
The isomorphism $\mathfrak{so}(3)\cong\mathfrak{su}(2)$
established in the previous subsection provides a structural
motivation that is absent for other gauge groups.
A second ordering step $O(3)\to O(2)$ would yield two broken and one
unbroken generator in the residual orientation sector.
This is only a counting-level and algebraic analogy to the electroweak
pattern; it is not by itself a derivation of the
$SU(2)\times U(1)\to U(1)_{\rm em}$ mechanism or of the observed
$W^\pm/Z/\gamma$ spectrum.

A structural candidate mechanism for such a second transition is
described in Appendix~\ref{app:second_transition}; it requires
two additional assumptions (phase--orientation coupling and
potential alignment) not yet derived from P1--P5.

\paragraph{Embedding model.}
A convenient electroweak-like EFT embedding is to introduce a
condensate doublet
\begin{equation}
  \Phi =
  \begin{pmatrix} \Phi_1 \\ \Phi_2 \end{pmatrix},
\end{equation}
with gauge-covariant dynamics
\begin{equation}
  S_{\text{weak}} = \int d^4X\left[
    \frac{1}{2}|D_A\Phi|^2
    + \frac{\lambda_2}{4}\left(\Phi^\dagger\Phi-v_2^2\right)^2
  \right],
\end{equation}
where
\begin{equation}
  D_A = \partial_A - ig\,\frac{\tau^a}{2}\,W_A^a - ig'\,Y\,B_A.
\end{equation}
Here $\tau^a$ are the Pauli matrices, $W_A^a$ the $SU(2)$ gauge
fields, $B_A$ the hypercharge gauge field, $Y$ the hypercharge
assignment, and $g$, $g'$ the corresponding coupling constants.
This construction is \emph{not} derived from the minimal scalar ECT
postulates P1--P5.
Rather, it provides a low-energy \emph{structural embedding} showing
how an electroweak-like EFT could be accommodated if a second
condensate transition exists.

\paragraph{Scale matching and the hierarchy problem.}
If such an intermediate transition is realised, the associated scale
is fixed by low-energy matching:
\begin{equation}
  v_2 = \left(\sqrt{2}\,G_F\right)^{-1/2}
  \approx 246.22\,\text{GeV},
\end{equation}
equivalently by the observed relations
\begin{equation}
  m_W = \frac{gv_2}{2}, \qquad
  m_Z = \frac{\sqrt{g^2+g'^2}}{2}\,v_2.
\end{equation}
Thus $246\,$GeV is not a free choice once the electroweak EFT is
matched.
The ratio $v_2/u_0\approx 10^{-16}$ is the ECT counterpart of the
standard gauge hierarchy problem, discussed in
Section~\ref{sec:three_scales}.

\paragraph{ECT interpretation: second phase of the same medium.}
If the second transition is realised, ECT would then interpret the
electroweak condensate not as an independent scalar field introduced
\emph{ad hoc}, but as a possible second ordered phase of the same underlying
$\Phi$-medium whose first transition already produced the Lorentzian
spacetime and the gravitational sector.
This is a conceptual distinction from the Standard Model, where the
Higgs field and the spacetime metric are independent structures.

At the current level of ECT this should be read only as follows:
\begin{itemize}
  \item the residual symmetry structure is \emph{compatible} with an
    electroweak-like embedding;
  \item the value of $v_2$ is a \emph{matching constraint} if that
    embedding is realised;
  \item the dynamical origin of the second transition remains
    open~(OP-EW; see Section~\ref{sec:three_scales} and
    Appendix~\ref{app:second_transition}).
\end{itemize}
Accordingly, the electroweak-like sector is currently a Level~B/C
structural embedding, not a first-principles derivation from the bare
scalar condensate action.

\subsection{Gauge sector summary, structural observations,
and the open programme}
\label{sec:gauge_summary}

\textit{Status: structural compatibility statement, Level~B/C.
The Standard Model gauge group $U(1)\times SU(2)\times SU(3)$ is not
derived from minimal ECT; it is shown to be embeddable if the
condensate carries the appropriate internal structure.
A full first-principles derivation remains open~(OP-SM).}

\paragraph{Separation of spacetime and internal symmetries.}
The spacetime symmetry breaking $O(4)\to O(3)$ acts on the coordinate
index of the Euclidean arena, whereas internal gauge symmetries act on
multiplet indices of the condensate components.
These sectors are therefore structurally distinct from the outset.

The Coleman--Mandula theorem~\cite{ColemanMandula1967} constrains the
structure of exact symmetries of a nontrivial relativistic S-matrix
under specific assumptions.
The present ECT discussion is formulated at the level of the condensate
action and its symmetry structure, prior to any full S-matrix
reconstruction.
For that reason, the theorem does not by itself settle the viability of
the present embedding programme.
This should not be read as a violation or circumvention of
Coleman--Mandula, but simply as a reminder that the current analysis is
outside its standard scope.

\paragraph{Connection to chirality.}
The group-theoretic decomposition $SO(4)\simeq SU(2)_L\times SU(2)_R$,
together with the SSB selection of a preferred axis, is discussed in the
context of fermion chirality in Section~\ref{sec:chirality}.
That discussion indicates how the symmetry breaking may structurally
distinguish the two $SU(2)$ sectors and motivate the selection of
$SU(2)_L$ as the surviving gauge factor~(Level~B structural reconstruction; fermions
are not derived from the bare bosonic condensate).

\paragraph{What is needed for Level~A.}
To promote the gauge sector from structural embedding to
first-principles derivation, the following programme would need to be
completed:
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)]
  \item derive the \emph{necessity} of internal multiplet structure
    from the condensate dynamics, rather than introducing it as an
    extension;
  \item derive the specific gauge group
    $U(1)\times SU(2)\times SU(3)$, or show that it is uniquely
    selected by the condensate structure;
  \item derive the coupling constants $(e, g, g', g_s)$ from
    condensate parameters;
  \item derive the second transition $O(3)\to O(2)$ dynamically
    from the bare action;
  \item show that the resulting EFT reproduces the full Standard
    Model gauge sector, including anomaly cancellation and the
    correct fermion representations.
\end{enumerate}
None of these steps has been completed.
The gauge sector is a structural embedding programme, not a
completed derivation.

\paragraph{Status summary.}

\begin{center}

  gauge theories
  & B/C & Embedding, not derivation \\

  & A & Direct Lie-algebra analogue in the

  & B & Standard EFT choice at leading derivative order \\

  & Matched & Not derived from condensate dynamics \\

  medium
  & B/C & Structural interpretation \\
